target_text: Tehran
description: Officials from the United Arab Emirates condemned the attacks by Iran, calling them a "flagrant violation of national sovereignty and international law", while stating the country has a right to protect itself and its civilians. Safety measures were implemented including airspace closures and public safety advisories, and urged residents to rely on official sources for information. Following the ongoing attacks by Iran, the UAE closed its embassy in Tehran and withdrew its ambassador and all diplomatic staff. The UAE reasserted its right to self-defense, rejecting accusations by Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi. A UAE official also stated that they would be open to mediation only if Iran stops the attacks.

target_text: Beginning at 09:05 UTC, Iran launched ballistic missiles towards Israel and seve
description: Beginning at 09:05 UTC, Iran launched ballistic missiles towards Israel and several Gulf states in retaliation to the American and Israeli strikes. At 10:15 UTC, missile strikes were confirmed in Israel. Strikes were reported in Haifa and Tel Aviv. A strike on a residential area in Tel Aviv killed a civilian woman in her forties and injured 27 others.

target_text: A Shahed-type drone reportedly struck near the Fairmont The Palm Hotel on Palm J
description: A Shahed-type drone reportedly struck near the Fairmont The Palm Hotel on Palm Jumeirah, causing a large explosion and fire. Four individuals were injured, primarily from debris and blast effects. The attack disrupted the luxurious district, with windows shattering in nearby buildings. The UAE said that it had intercepted a "new wave" of Iranian missiles and that "fragments from the interceptions" had fallen in Abu Dhabi and Dubai, causing damage to Burj Al Arab.

target_text: A spokesperson for the Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces says that two of its fi
description: A spokesperson for the Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces says that two of its fighters had been killed while three more had been injured during strikes in Jurf al-Sakhar, south of Baghdad.

target_text: After the missile and drone attacks on Kuwait, its foreign ministry summoned Moh
description: After the missile and drone attacks on Kuwait, its foreign ministry summoned Mohammad Toutounchi, Iran's ambassador in Kuwait.

target_text: Al Minhad Air Base — Australian/Coalition Logistics Hub
description: Iranian drones struck Al Minhad Air Base — a logistics hub for Australia's Middle East operations located approximately 25km south of Dubai — on the first night of the Iran conflict (Feb 28). Australian Defence Minister Richard Marles confirmed the strike on March 3, stating all Australian Defence Force (ADF) personnel stationed at the base were safe and uninjured. Marles: 'We have a number of Australians who operate from a headquarters that we've had at Al Minhad now for many, many years.' Al Minhad hosts coalition forces including ADF and has served as a key staging post for Middle East operations for over a decade. Strike was confirmed only publicly on March 3 — the event itself occurred Feb 28 as part of Iran's first-night retaliatory strikes across the UAE and broader Gulf region.
source_urls: Australian Defence Minister Richard Marles (ABC News Australia — March 3 2026) | The Guardian (Australia — March 3 2026) | 7NEWS Australia (March 3 2026) | InDaily South Australia (March 3 2026)

target_text: Al Udeid Air Base — Iranian Missile Strike
description: Iran targeted the primary US air operations hub in the Gulf. IRGC confirmed targeting al-Udeid. Qatar defence ministry said it 'thwarted' several attacks. Part of Operation True Promise 4.

target_text: Al-Dhafra Air Base — Iranian Missile Strike
description: Iran targeted Al-Dhafra Air Base, hosting ~5,000 US military personnel near Abu Dhabi. IRGC confirmed targeting the base. UAE air defence intercepted multiple missiles and drones.

target_text: Ali al-Salem Air Base — Iranian Missile Strike
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles at Ali al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait. Kuwait air defence intercepted all missiles. A drone also crashed into Kuwait International Airport, wounding several employees with limited damage. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers injured.

target_text: Bahrain High-Rise — Iranian Drone Strike
description: Iranian drone flew directly into a high-rise building in Manama in what appeared to be a targeted attack. Building engulfed in flames. Bahrain confirmed drone attacks and falling debris hit 3 buildings in Manama and Muharraq.
source_urls: BBC | Guardian | Bahrain Ministry of Interior

target_text: Bushehr — Adjacent Military Facilities
description: Explosions reported near Bushehr port city but NO confirmed direct strikes on reactor. Rosatom evacuated ~100 staff. Significant radiological and diplomatic risk (Russian personnel on-site). Per CSIS: unclear if dismantling reactor is a US/Israel priority.

target_text: Chabahar Port — Military Facilities
description: Explosions reported near Chabahar port, Iran's only deep-water port on the Indian Ocean.

target_text: Collateral Damage — Missile Debris
description: 5 killed and several wounded in Suwayda, Syria from missile debris or collateral damage during Iranian strikes.

target_text: Damavand — Military Targets
description: Strikes reported in Damavand, east of Tehran. Known location of underground missile storage facilities.

target_text: Girls Primary School
description: Strike hit a girls primary school (Shajareh Tayyebeh school, Minab, Hormozgan Province). Death toll rose from initial 36 → 51 → 57 → 85 → 115 → 148 → now 165 killed per IRNA/Guardian (March 1, ~3 PM ET) citing Iranian state media; IRNA also cites local prosecutor at 165. 96 wounded (updated from 95). Children aged 7-12. School located next to IRGC barracks per Guardian. Washington Post verified footage of destroyed school. ~170 students present during class period change at 10:45 IRST. Perpetrator disputed — Iran attributes to US-Israel airstrike; other claims unresolved. Verified by NYT, Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, Guardian, IRNA.
source_urls: Al Jazeera | Reuters | AP | Tasnim | NPR

target_text: Hamad International Airport
description: According to the Associated Press, Doha's Hamad International Airport experienced disruptions following the closure of Qatari airspace on 28 February 2026. Flight cancellations were reported as a result, and countries such as Israel and the United Arab Emirates closed their respective airspaces the same day. On 7 March, the Qatar Civil Aviation Authority (QCAA) announced a reopening of air navigation under limited emergency conditions, allowing for passenger evacuation and cargo flights, with standard commercial flights remaining suspended.

target_text: IRGC Headquarters
description: Coordinated US-Israel strike on IRGC central command. Several senior IRGC commanders reported killed.

target_text: IRIS Shahid Bagheri — IRGCN Drone Carrier, Bandar Abbas Roads
description: US forces struck Iranian IRGC Navy drone carrier IRIS Shahid Bagheri (C110-4) in the Bandar Abbas roads (approximately 4 nautical miles south of the harbor) during the opening hours of Operation Epic Fury on Feb 28. CENTCOM confirmed on March 2: 'The Iranian regime's false messaging machine continues to falsely claim that it has sunk a US aircraft carrier. The only carrier that has been hit is the Shahid Bagheri, an Iranian drone carrier. US forces struck the ship within hours of launching Operation Epic Fury.' The Shahid Bagheri is a converted container ship with an angled flight deck and ski-jump designed for drone operations — converted from the container ship Perarin in 2022-24. Its sister ship IRIS Shahid Mahdavi (C110-3) was also anchored nearby. Final status of the vessel (sunk/damaged) not yet confirmed by CENTCOM beyond 'struck.'
source_urls: US CENTCOM official statement (March 2, 2026) | CBS News Day 3 liveblog (March 2, 2026) | Times of Israel liveblog (March 2, 2026) | MarineInsight (vessel position data)

target_text: Ilam — Military Targets
description: Strikes reported in Ilam, western Iran near Iraqi border. IRGC facilities in the area.

target_text: Iran Atomic Energy Agency Headquarters
description: Unconfirmed reports of US strikes on IAEA headquarters in Tehran. Part of targeting peripheral nuclear capabilities and administrative hubs.

target_text: Iranian Missile Hits US Base
description: | video1 = [https://www.youtube.com/shorts/WuVZdCFOj9c Moment Iranian Missile Hits US Base in Bahrain]

target_text: Isfahan
description: The Fars News Agency and CNN reported more explosions across other cities in Iran, including Qom, Kermanshah, Isfahan, and Karaj. In Tehran, missile strikes were reported to have occurred on University Street, in the Jomhouri area, and in the northern Seyed Khandan area. At 07:15 UTC, The Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school in Minab, Iran, is struck by a missile. Iranian media reported 180 killed. A sports hall in Lamerd was bombed during a girls' practice, killing at least 18 civilians, according to witnesses and Iranian state media. The IRGC reports that the port city of Bushehr had also been struck; however, it was unclear whether the nuclear reactor had sustained any damage. Rafael Mariano Grossi, the U.N. nuclear watchdog chief stated that no evidence has been found that nuclear facilities have been hit from these attacks.

target_text: Israel declared a state of emergency, citing expectation of an Iranian attack. S
description: Israel declared a state of emergency, citing expectation of an Iranian attack. Sirens blared in Israel as the government warned its citizens to remain in protected areas. The Israeli Ministry of Health moved its hospital operations underground. Israel called up 20,000 reservists in addition to the 50,000 already on duty.

target_text: Israel warned Iranian civilians residing near military industries and infrastruc
description: Israel warned Iranian civilians residing near military industries and infrastructure to evacuate immediately, stating "Your presence in these areas puts your life at risk." The attacks also used about 200 Israeli fighter jets, in the largest combat sortie in Israel's history, and the newly developed Black Sparrow air-launched ballistic missile, fired from F-15s.

target_text: Jordan said that its armed forces had shot down two Iranian ballistic missiles t
description: Jordan said that its armed forces had shot down two Iranian ballistic missiles targeting its territory and that it had handled 54 reports of falling debris that caused material damage but no casualties.

target_text: Karaj — Military Targets
description: Strikes on Karaj, industrial city west of Tehran. Home to centrifuge production facilities.

target_text: Kataib Hezbollah Base
description: US airstrikes on Iraqi military base housing Kataib Hezbollah southeast of Baghdad. 4 explosions heard. 2 killed per AFP.

target_text: Kermanshah — Military Targets
description: Strikes on Kermanshah in western Iran near the Iraqi border. IRGC facilities in the area.

target_text: Khamenei Compound
description: Israel's opening strike. Airbus satellite imagery confirms multiple destroyed/heavily damaged structures within the compound. Khamenei evacuated beforehand.

target_text: Kuwait International Airport — Iranian Drone Strike
description: Iranian drone crashed into Kuwait International Airport, wounding several employees. Limited damage to terminal. Separate from strikes on Ali al-Salem Air Base.

target_text: Mehrabad Air Base
description: Israeli F-35s struck this major military airfield in western Tehran. Runway cratered, hangars destroyed.

target_text: Ministry of Intelligence
description: Separate NYT-verified video captures strike near Iran's Ministry of Intelligence. Part of second wave targeting command infrastructure.

target_text: Narmak Neighborhood (Ahmadinejad residence area)
description: NYT-verified: Strike hit 72nd Square in Narmak district (northeastern Tehran). Former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad confirmed killed here — Iranian state media (ILNA), Xinhua, Guardian, Reuters (March 1, 2026). Strike also struck Hedayat High School — 2 students confirmed killed per NYT (citing Mehr news agency). Buildings and cars ablaze. Second school struck during the conflict.

target_text: On 28 February 2026, 16 people were injured by shrapnel following the intercepti
description: On 28 February 2026, 16 people were injured by shrapnel following the interception of an Iranian missile barrage by Qatari air defenses. According to the head of Qatar's Ministry of the Interior, 66 missiles were fired in total and there were over 100 reports of falling shrapnel across the nation. The Ministry later alerted the public of the attack.

target_text: On 28 February 2026, a joint missile operation was coordinated by the United Sta
description: On 28 February 2026, a joint missile operation was coordinated by the United States and Israel, striking multiple targets in Iran. US President Donald Trump stated that the strikes were part of an effort to initiate a regime change among other goals including those related to the halting of Iran's nuclear program. Following the strikes, the Iranian government retaliated across the Persian Gulf, with airstrikes hitting countries across the region.

target_text: On 28 February 2026, following the coordinated 2026 Israeli–United States strike
description: On 28 February 2026, following the coordinated 2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran, the Islamic Republic of Iran launched a multiday series of missile and drone airstrikes on the United Arab Emirates. By 17 March, Iran had launched 314 ballistic missiles, 1,672 drone attacks and 15 cruise missiles (according to UAE ministry) to targets in the UAE. The attacks have killed 8 people, including 2 military personnel, and injured 157 others.

target_text: On 28 February 2026, following the initial strikes during the 2026 Iran war, Ira
description: On 28 February 2026, following the initial strikes during the 2026 Iran war, Iran conducted retaliatory missile and drone strikes on Bahrain, hitting multiple buildings in the capital Manama including the district of Juffair, where the US Navy's 5th Fleet is headquartered at Naval Support Activity Bahrain.

target_text: Parchin Military Complex
description: Explosive research testing facility struck. Confirmed by CSIS and Middle East Forum. Vision analysis of Telegram video shows munitions cookoff consistent with weapons storage. Known facility for missile component manufacturing and explosives testing.
source_urls: War Monitor | Vahid Online | CSIS | MEF

target_text: Presidential Palace & National Security Council
description: NYT-verified video shows strikes hitting area containing presidential palace, National Security Council, and key government buildings. Second wave of strikes.

target_text: Prince Sultan Air Base — Iranian Missile Strike
description: Iran fired short-range missiles at Prince Sultan Air Base near Riyadh. Saudi Arabia stated it 'will take all necessary measures to defend its security.' IRGC confirmed targeting the base. Per CSIS/Al Arabiya.

target_text: Qom — Military/Government Targets
description: Strikes on Qom, a major religious and political center. Specific targets TBC.

target_text: Shiraz — Southern Command Hub
description: Strikes on Shiraz, a major military hub in southern Iran. Home to IRGC air force installations.

target_text: Sultan's Pool / Near Temple Mount — Iranian Ballistic Missile Warhead Impact
description: The warhead of an Iranian ballistic missile impacted Sultan's Pool, an outdoor events venue located just a few dozen meters west of the Old City of Jerusalem — less than one kilometer from the Temple Mount and Western Wall. Israel Police discovered the warhead and 'incendiary and explosive materials' scattered nearby during searches. The explosion was heard in the area on Saturday shortly after the US-Israeli strikes on Iran commenced, and smoke was seen rising from the site. First confirmed Iranian missile impact this close to Jerusalem's Old City.
source_urls: Israel Police (official statement) | Times of Israel liveblog March 1 2026

target_text: Tabriz Military Airfield
description: Israeli strike on northwestern Iran airfield. Hangar and fuel storage damage reported.

target_text: US Navy 5th Fleet HQ — Iranian Missile Strike
description: Iranian missile struck the headquarters of the US Navy's 5th Fleet in Manama, Bahrain. Huge plumes of black smoke seen rising from the area. IRGC confirmed targeting the base. Bahrain confirmed the attack.

target_text: Urmia — Military Targets
description: Strikes reported in Urmia, far northwest Iran near Turkey border. Specific targets TBC.
source_urls: Al Jazeera | Fars News | Indian Express

target_text: Vehicles & Buildings — Residential Damage
description: Footage shows flames engulfing cars and buildings destroyed in Tehran. Collateral damage from nearby strikes.

target_text: Zanjan — Ballistic Missile Infrastructure
description: Massive explosion in Zanjan, northwestern Iran. Ballistic missile launching infrastructure struck. Enormous fireball visible.

target_text: Zayed International Airport — Iranian Missile Strike
description: Iranian ballistic missile debris struck Zayed International Airport in Abu Dhabi. 1 killed (Pakistani national), 7 injured. Abu Dhabi Airports confirmed the incident then deleted the post.

target_text: and drones toward US base
description: Iran launched the first wave of ballistic missiles and drones toward US bases in Abu Dhabi, including Al Dhafra Air Base. UAE defences intercepted most, but debris fell in a residential area near Zayed International Airport, killing one civilian (a Pakistani national) and injuring seven others from shrapnel and blasts. Loud explosions shook the Corniche, Al Dhafra, and Bateen districts, with smoke rising over the city. This strike targeted US forces at Al Dhafra, causing minor structural damage to base facilities from secondary explosions.

target_text: attack on US military base
description: Bahrain activated air-raid sirens to warn of an Iranian attack on US military bases within the nation, with Arabic media saying explosions and smoke were seen in the capital, Manama. thumb|Drone attacks and strikes in the UAEExplosions were reported at Kuwait International Airport and Abu Dhabi in the United Arab Emirates. There were missile and drone attacks on Kuwait. Saudi Arabia confirmed that there had been Iranian attacks on Riyadh and Eastern Province. It claimed to have successfully intercepted Iranian attacks aimed at those areas and said that the Kingdom will "take all necessary measures" to defend itself, "including the option of responding to aggression". Residential areas of Dubai in the proximity of the Burj Khalifa, Dubai Marina and the Dubai Palm were hit by strikes, setting the Fairmont The Palm hotel on fire and causing four injuries; a residential building on the outskirts of Doha was also hit. Local sources said Iran used Shahed drones, which are among the weapons most used by Iran and its proxies, including Hamas and Hezbollah. The UAE said that it had intercepted a "new wave" of Iranian missiles and that "fragments from the interceptions" had fallen in Abu Dhabi and Dubai, causing damage to Burj Al Arab. Strikes on Kuwait International Airport caused several undefined injuries. Qatar says that it had intercepted at least two waves of missile attacks, stating that no casualties or property damage had been reported.

target_text: flights from Hamad International Airport
description: Since the 2026 Iran war began with United States and Israeli strikes on Iran on 28 February 2026, following the breakdown of US-Iran talks and negotiations, locations across Qatar have been subject to multiple retaliatory Iranian missile strikes. Qatar's airspace was closed the day of the first strikes, disrupting flights from Hamad International Airport in the capital, Doha. The Qatari government reported that it had shot down two Iranian Su-24 bombers on 2 March 2026, and several missiles have been intercepted.

target_text: had targeted four US base
description: The IRGC said that Iran had targeted four US bases in the Middle East: Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar; Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait (which also hosted Italian soldiers); Al Dhafra Air Base in the UAE; and the US Navy Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain.

target_text: including Erbil International Airport
description: In the Kurdistan Region of Iraq, Iranian aerial attacks targeted infrastructure hosting US military and civilian personnel, including Erbil International Airport and the US Consulate General in Erbil. According to local media reports, most missiles and drones were intercepted.

target_text: shortly before Dubai International Airport
description: thumb|An emergency alert sent to UAE residents amidst the Iranian strikes. This specific one was sent shortly before Dubai International Airport was struck at around 12:30 A.M. local time.

description: * Israeli attack on Doha

target_text: AWS reported that, at 4:30 AM Pacific Standard Time (4:30 PM Gulf Standard Time)
description: AWS reported that, at 4:30 AM Pacific Standard Time (4:30 PM Gulf Standard Time), one of its data centers in mec1-az2 was on fire after being struck by 'objects' and the power was then shut down. Later that day, AWS reported 'localised power issues' in az3.

target_text: An Iranian missile breached Israeli defences and hit a synagogue and homes in Be
description: An Iranian missile breached Israeli defences and hit a synagogue and homes in Beit Shemesh, killing nine people and injuring 65. Towards the end of the day, an Iranian ballistic missile hit an area of Jerusalem, causing property damage in the surrounding road, damaging a civilian vehicle and wounding six people.

target_text: At 06:35 UTC, CENTCOM announced that it "and partner forces" had begun airstrike
description: At 06:35 UTC, CENTCOM announced that it "and partner forces" had begun airstrikes against Iran. US warships launched Tomahawk missiles, while the US Army used HIMARS launchers. Undisclosed long-range standoff weapons were also used. At 06:45 UTC, the Israeli Air Force began an unprecedented wave of decapitation strikes. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several high officials (attending three meetings at his residential compound) were killed, as were members of his family. The United States Navy, United States Air Force and United States Marine Corps focused primarily on command-and-control infrastructure, missiles, UAV sites, and radar installations.

target_text: Beit Shemesh — Iranian Ballistic Missile Direct Hit
description: An Iranian ballistic missile scored a direct hit in Beit Shemesh, central Israel. Toll rose from initial 4 → 6 → 8 → 9 killed as rescuers extracted additional bodies from rubble. 20+ injured, including a 4-year-old boy (moderate trauma) and multiple others hospitalized. Strike destroyed a synagogue, damaged a public bomb shelter, and heavily damaged surrounding homes. Mayor: 20 residents still unreachable. IDF investigating failure to intercept. IRGC claimed strike as part of Operation Honest Promise 4.
source_urls: Magen David Adom (official) | Times of Israel liveblog | Haaretz liveblog

target_text: Border Regiment Headquarters
description: Attack on Iranian border regiment headquarters in Mehran, Ilam Province — western Iran near Iraqi border. Iranian state media (Mehr) reports 43 security personnel killed, most of them border guards; nearby buildings also seriously damaged. Iran attributes attack to US and Israel. No US/Israeli confirmation of this specific strike. Location near Iraq border; attacker not independently verified.
source_urls: Mehr News Agency (Iran) | Times of Israel | France 24 | New Indian Express

target_text: Crowne Plaza Hotel — Iranian Strike
description: Iranian strike struck the Crowne Plaza Hotel in Manama, Bahrain on March 1. US Embassy Manama officially confirmed the strike and warned US citizens to avoid hotels in the capital as potential targets. AFP correspondent on the ground saw damage at the hotel after hearing explosions. Following this strike, US government personnel were moved out of hotels across Bahrain. Injuries confirmed; number unclear.

target_text: Duqm Commercial Port — Iranian Drone Strike
description: Iranian drones targeted Duqm commercial port in southeastern Oman, wounding one port worker. First confirmed Iranian strike on Omani soil. Oman's state news agency (ONA) confirmed the incident. Duqm is a major deep-water port and Special Economic Zone on Oman's Arabian Sea coast. Oman had previously maintained a neutral posture and served as an intermediary in Iran-US negotiations.
source_urls: Oman News Agency (ONA) | Times of Israel liveblog

target_text: Etihad Towers / Union Towers — Day 2 Iranian Drone Debris
description: Day 2 of Iranian strikes on Abu Dhabi. Debris from an intercepted drone fell onto Etihad Towers — a luxury high-rise complex housing the Conrad Hotel — causing minor injuries to a woman and her child and minor material damage. Shrapnel from interceptions also struck the Union Towers building in central Abu Dhabi. Follows the previous day's fatal strike on Zayed International Airport that killed 1 and injured 7. UAE airspace remains closed; Etihad Airways suspended flights until at least 2am local Monday.
source_urls: Abu Dhabi Media Office (official X post) | CNBC

target_text: Fallen debris caused damage to structures in Dubai, including areas around Palm
description: Fallen debris caused damage to structures in Dubai, including areas around Palm Jumeirah and the Burj Al Arab hotel. In the residential and commercial areas of Abu Dhabi debris caused damage to property killing one civilian and injuring several others.

target_text: Following the high tensions and military conflict that began during late 2023, t
description: Following the high tensions and military conflict that began during late 2023, the ongoing tensions between Iran and the US, the reinstatement of economic sanctions and Iran and the 2025-2026 Iranian protests and massacres, the US started a military buildup in the Middle East, with the aim for regime change in Iran. In late February, an Israeli American coordinated air strike was launched against Iran, targeting its leaders and main military infrastructures and air defence systems. In retaliation Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones against several countries around the Middle East that included the UAE, Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Kuwait, and Israel.

target_text: French Naval Air Base (FFEAU / Camp de la Paix) — Port of Abu Dhabi
description: An Iranian drone struck a hangar at France's permanent naval air base in the UAE (FFEAU — French Forces United Arab Emirates, also known as Camp de la Paix), located near the Port of Abu Dhabi adjacent to an Emirati military base. French President Macron confirmed the strike in remarks to France's National Defense Council on Sunday: 'The hangar at the French base, which is adjacent to the Emirati base, was hit in a drone attack targeting the Port of Abu Dhabi.' No casualties — 'limited to material losses' per Macron. France has ~700 troops permanently stationed at this facility. First confirmed Iranian strike on a permanent French military installation. Macron said this 'requires us to strengthen our posture and our defensive support.' France, Germany, and UK subsequently issued a joint warning of potential 'defensive action to destroy Iran's capability to fire missiles and drones at their source.'
source_urls: French President Emmanuel Macron (National Defense Council address) | CBS News

target_text: IRIN Frigate Jamaran — Sunk at Chabahar Pier
description: US CENTCOM confirmed it sank a Jamaran-class Iranian corvette (frigate Jamaran, built 2010, ~1,500-ton displacement) at a pier in Chabahar port in the Gulf of Oman. CENTCOM stated the ship is 'currently sinking to the bottom of the Gulf of Oman.' CENTCOM simultaneously called on all Iranian armed forces to 'lay down your weapons — abandon ship,' citing Trump's call for Iran's military to stand down. First confirmed US sinking of an Iranian Navy surface combatant in the conflict. IRIN frigate Jamaran was Iran's most capable domestically-built warship.
source_urls: US CENTCOM (official X statement) | Reuters | MarketScreener

target_text: Iran launched retaliatory missile and drone strikes against Israel, UAE, Qatar,
description: Iran launched retaliatory missile and drone strikes against Israel, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia. Iran claimed to have shot down an American MQ-9 Reaper drone. The IRGC claimed that it struck with four missiles, which was later denied by a US official.

target_text: Iranian Ballistic Missile Launchers & Air Defense Systems
description: Israel launched a new wave of strikes on Sunday March 1 targeting Iranian ballistic missile launchers and air defense systems across central Iran, as the conflict entered its second day following Khamenei's confirmed death. Strikes described as aimed at dismantling Iran's remaining offensive and defensive capabilities.

target_text: Iranian Strikes Hit Dubai Airport
description: | video2 = [https://www.youtube.com/shorts/vbiGygZ0hnE Iranian Strikes Hit Dubai Airport]

target_text: Iranian attacks killed one person and injured 32 in Kuwait.
description: Iranian attacks killed one person and injured 32 in Kuwait.

target_text: Jebel Ali Port — Fire from Iranian Missile/Drone Debris
description: Fire broke out at one of the berths of Jebel Ali Port — one of the world's busiest container ports — following Iranian missile and drone strikes on Dubai. Dubai authorities stated the fire was caused by 'debris resulting from air interception operations.' No injuries reported. Dubai Civil Defence responded immediately. Reuters Connect published photographs of smoke billowing from the port. CNBC and AFP imagery confirmed visible smoke plume from the harbor.
source_urls: Reuters Connect | Indian Express | CNBC | Dubai Civil Defence

target_text: Kataeb Hezbollah Fighters — al-Qaim Border Crossing
description: Two fighters from Kataeb Hezbollah killed in an airstrike near the al-Qaim border crossing in western Iraq, near the Iraq-Syria border. Two Kataeb sources told AFP: 'Two fighters from Kataeb Hezbollah were martyred in an American-Israeli bombing near the al-Qaim border crossing.' A separate Kataeb group official confirmed the toll to AFP. Al-Qaim (Anbar Province) is a key corridor used by Iranian-backed militias to move fighters and weapons between Iraq and Syria. Strike attributed to the US-Israel coalition; Iranian-backed proxy forces targeted as part of the wider regional campaign.
source_urls: AFP (two Kataeb Hezbollah sources) | Kataeb Hezbollah group official (third confirmation via AFP) | Times of Israel liveblog

target_text: MV Skylight — Oil Tanker Attack & Sinking
description: Palau-flagged oil tanker MV Skylight struck approximately 5 nautical miles off Oman's Musandam peninsula at the mouth of the Strait of Hormuz. 4 crew injured, all 20 crew evacuated. UPDATED March 1 ~11:30 AM ET: Iranian state TV (Mehr News Agency) and Defense News report the vessel is now SINKING after the strike, having attempted 'unauthorized passage' through the IRGC-closed Strait. The Defense News cites Omani maritime authorities confirming fire and the vessel beginning to sink. BBC confirms Iranian state TV claim but has not independently verified. First tanker sinking of the conflict. IRGC naval campaign ongoing; Strait of Hormuz closed to all non-Iranian/Chinese shipping.
source_urls: Oman Maritime Security Centre | Times of Israel | Moneycontrol | MarketScreener | Iranian state TV / Mehr News | The Defense News | BBC

target_text: Marshall Islands-Flagged Crude Tanker — Struck by Projectile
description: A Marshall Islands-flagged crude oil tanker was struck by a projectile approximately 44.4 nautical miles northwest of Muscat, Oman, while sailing with cargo. Confirmed by two maritime security sources. Separate and distinct from the earlier MV Skylight attack (Palau-flagged, near Musandam). Part of ongoing IRGC naval campaign amid Strait of Hormuz closure. Crew status and damage details pending.
source_urls: Times of Israel liveblog | Maritime security sources (x2)

target_text: Natanz Nuclear Enrichment Facility (AEOI/IRGC)
description: Iran's IAEA ambassador Reza Najafi confirmed Natanz nuclear enrichment facility was struck in US-Israeli operations. Speaking at the IAEA's extraordinary 35-nation board of governors meeting in Vienna (called at Russia's request), Najafi told Reuters: 'Again they attacked Iran's peaceful, safeguarded nuclear facilities yesterday' — and when asked which facilities, replied: 'Natanz.' The IAEA Director General Grossi separately said 'Up to now, the agency has no indication that any of the nuclear installations have been damaged or hit in Iran' — but warned it 'cannot rule out a possible radiological release with serious consequences.' Natanz is Iran's primary uranium enrichment facility and was previously struck in Operation Midnight Hammer (June 2025). No confirmed casualties or radiological release as of reporting.
source_urls: Reuters (Francois Murphy — on-site at IAEA Board of Governors emergency session, March 2 2026) | Washington Post (March 2 2026) | ABPLive/ANI citing Reuters (March 2 2026) | UK House of Commons Library research briefing (March 2 2026)

target_text: Northern Israel — Hezbollah Missile & Projectile Barrage
description: Hezbollah launched a large wave of rockets, precision missiles, and drones toward northern Israel — the first major attack from Lebanon since the US-Israel strikes on Iran began on February 28. Hezbollah specifically claimed targeting the Mishmar Karmel missile defense site of the Israeli army south of Haifa, stating it used 'a barrage of precision missiles and a swarm of drones' against the site. At least six rockets landed in the Haifa area. IDF activated air raid sirens across northern Israel and several areas near Haifa. Hezbollah said the strikes were 'in retaliation for the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and for repeated Israeli aggressions.' Wikipedia lists Hezbollah as a belligerent 'Since 2 March' (UTC). Israel responded immediately with a barrage of airstrikes on Beirut (see s068). Marks a significant expansion of the conflict — Hezbollah opening a northern front from Lebanon with attacks reaching the Haifa area.
source_urls: Israeli Defense Forces (IDF siren activation — northern Israel) | Reuters liveblog (11:36 PM UTC March 1, 2026) | AP News (March 2, 2026 — 'Israel launches barrage of strikes on Lebanese capital') | Wikipedia (2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran — Hezbollah listed as belligerent 'Since 2 March')

target_text: RAF Akrotiri — Suspected Iranian Drone Strike
description: An Iranian drone struck RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus (a UK Sovereign Base Area) at approximately midnight local time (22:00 UTC March 1). UK Ministry of Defence confirmed: 'Our Armed Forces are responding to a suspected drone strike at RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus at midnight local time. Our force protection in the region is at the highest level and the base has responded to defend our people. This is a live situation.' Minor structural damage to the airfield; no casualties. ~2,000 service personnel and their families were ordered to take cover and stay away from windows. Flight trackers showed RAF jets scrambled to defend the base. The strike came within approximately one hour of UK PM Keir Starmer granting the US permission to launch 'defensive' strikes on Iranian missile sites from UK bases — widely seen as Iranian retaliation for that announcement. Wikipedia: 'In the first hour of 2 March 2026 there were reports that RAF Akrotiri on the island of Cyprus has been hit by a drone causing minor structural damages.' First confirmed physical strike causing damage on a UK/European military installation in this conflict.
source_urls: UK Ministry of Defence (official spokesperson statement) | Daily Mail | Wikipedia (2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran — 'first hour of 2 March 2026') | GB News | Yahoo News / PA Media

target_text: Saudi oil refinery
description: Riyadh was again hit by direct strikes. A Saudi oil refinery was closed after being targeted by Iranian drones. Around 15:30 UTC, Saudi Arabia claimed that it had shot down missiles fired at Prince Sultan Airbase and King Khalid International Airport.

target_text: Sharjah Industrial Area — Warehouse Fire from Iranian Strike
description: Iranian strikes on the UAE caused a warehouse fire in Sharjah's industrial area, with thick black smoke billowing from the scene. Sharjah is the third-largest emirate in the UAE, adjacent to Dubai. UAE authorities confirmed the fire was contained with no casualties. AP imagery published in the NYT. ARN News Centre and Dubai Eye 103.8 both confirmed 'Sharjah warehouse fire contained with no casualties.' Part of Iran's broader wave of retaliatory strikes on UAE territory.
source_urls: AP (Altaf Qadri photograph — NYT) | ARN News Centre | Dubai Eye 103.8 | Associated Press video

target_text: Tabriz Airport — Iranian F4/F5 Fighter Jets Destroyed on Tarmac
description: Israeli Air Force struck two Iranian fighter jets — one F4 Phantom and one F5 Freedom Fighter — at Tabriz Airport in northwestern Iran as they were being prepared for takeoff. The IDF stated the strike was aimed at degrading the Iranian Air Force's operational capacity and preventing further retaliatory sorties. Tabriz is a major IRIAF base hub in western Iran. Confirmed by IDF statement and multiple independent outlets.
source_urls: IDF statement | Haaretz | India Today | Firstpost

target_text: Tehran
description: Israel executed another wave of strikes against Iran, The USAF flew B-2 Bombers to attack hardened ballistic missile facilities with 2,000 pound bombs. USN F/A-18s and USMC F-35s, aided by Tomahawk missiles were also employed along with Lucas drones. CENTCOM declared that it was instructed to "dismantle the Iranian regime's security apparatus." Israel and the United States launched strikes against targets in Tehran, Sanandaj, and military and strategic locations including command centers and missile sites. The Israel Defense Forces says that it bombed an F-4 and an F-5 fighter jet preparing to take off from a runway, and the HQ-9B system around Tehran was inactivated. The Tehran Revolutionary Court was bombed and destroyed. Explosions are also reported near the Azadi Stadium, Azadi Square, and Milad Tower. The Iranian Red Crescent Society reported explosions near hospitals in Tehran, as well as near its Peace Building. Mohammad Baseri, an official in the ministry of intelligence, and Gholamreza Rezaian, the head of the intelligence department of FARAJA police, were both killed in strikes on Iran.

target_text: The IDF proceeded with a series of strategic bombings on Beirut and the Beqaa Va
description: The IDF proceeded with a series of strategic bombings on Beirut and the Beqaa Valley. Israel warned Lebanese people who fled from their homes not to return. Civilians said they did not know how they would access food or water while displaced. The IDF later announced that it struck more than 70 targets in Dahieh and southern Lebanon. The IDF struck three southern Lebanese towns, including Haris, Nabatieh al-Fawqa, and Mayfadoun, and later issued an evacuation order in 50 adjacent communities. The IDF said it killed the head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters Hussein Makled in the strikes. In the night, it reported the killing of Abu Hamza Rami, the Palestinian Islamic Jihad commander in Lebanon, in Beirut.

target_text: The IRGC claimed that it struck the with four missiles. US CENTCOM has stated th
description: The IRGC claimed that it struck the with four missiles. US CENTCOM has stated that the carrier was not struck and asserted that it is fully operational.

target_text: UK Sovereign Base Areas — Iranian Missile Strike
description: UK Defence Secretary John Healey confirmed two Iranian missiles were fired in the direction of Cyprus, targeting the UK Sovereign Base Areas (SBAs). First confirmed Iranian missile attack directed toward European territory. Healey said 'We are pretty sure they weren't targeted at our bases,' but called it 'an example of a very real, rising threat.' 300 British troops were reported near Iranian strikes in Bahrain. British forces were actively intercepting Iranian drones threatening UK bases and allies across the region.
source_urls: UK Defence Secretary John Healey (Sky News) | The Independent | The Standard | Yahoo News | Times of Israel

target_text: UK military officials said an RAF Typhoon shot down an Iranian drone bound for Q
description: UK military officials said an RAF Typhoon shot down an Iranian drone bound for Qatari airspace. At about 22:00 UTC: a series of loud blasts were recorded in Doha, and continued to be heard hours later.

target_text: US use of British base
description: To avert further Iranian strikes, the E3 (the UK, France, and Germany) resolved to back, if needed, "proportionate military defensive measures" against drones and ballistic missiles. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces approval of US use of British bases for "defensive" strikes on Iran.

target_text: USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) — IRGC Claim DEBUNKED
description: IRGC claimed to have struck USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier in the Arabian Sea with 4 anti-ship ballistic missiles. CLAIM OFFICIALLY DENIED: US Central Command (CENTCOM) stated on Sunday March 1: 'The Lincoln was not hit. The missiles launched didn't even come close.' IRGC claim is debunked — the carrier was not struck.
source_urls: IRGC statement (via Iranian state media) | US CENTCOM (denial) | Eurasian Times | Times of Israel liveblog

target_text: Unidentified Vessel — Struck by Projectile near Mina Saqr
description: An unidentified vessel was struck by an unknown projectile approximately 17 nautical miles northwest of UAE's Mina Saqr port in Ras Al Khaimah. A fire broke out onboard but was extinguished and the vessel intended to continue its voyage. Confirmed by UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO). Third confirmed tanker/vessel attack of the conflict, following MV Skylight (Musandam) and the Marshall Islands-flagged crude tanker off Muscat.
source_urls: UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) | Times of Israel liveblog

target_text: on the natural gas facility
description: British prime minister Keir Starmer denounced the 18 March Iranian attack on the natural gas facility in Qatar.

target_text: plumes rising from the Port
description: Missiles from Iran were fired at Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain. Witnesses saw smoke from missile intercepts over Dubai and dark plumes rising from the Port of Jebel Ali, United Arab Emirates. In Abu Dhabi a drone was intercepted near the Etihad Towers complex, near the Israeli embassy. Upon being intercepted its debris damaged the towers and caused minor injuries to a woman and her child. The French naval base Camp de la Paix was reportedly struck by Iran. Two Iranian drones targeted a warehouse at Al-Salam Naval Base causing a fire in two containers storing general materials. Around 02:30 UTC, an Amazon Web Services data center within the UAE was "impacted by objects that struck" it, resulting in "sparks and fire". Reuters reported that this resulted in a complete power cut and shutdown of the center with the involvement of local fire department crews, leading to a temporary connectivity outage of the availability zone impacted.

target_text: the Al Udeid Air Base
description: CBC News, citing the Canadian Department of National Defence's website, reported that Canadian soldiers of the Combined Aerospace Operations Centre were present at the Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar at the time of the attack. On 3 March 2026, Canadian Defence Minister David McGuinty confirmed Canadian military presence in the Middle East and confirmed that they hadn't suffered any casualties. McGuinty also stated that the Canadian Armed Forces will “assess any potential impacts on CAF personnel in the region."

target_text: the port
description: During drone operations in the Strait of Hormuz, the port of Duqm in Oman was reportedly hit by two drones, injuring one expatriate worker. Oman's Maritime Security Centre confirmed that an oil tanker which was Palau-flagged, named Skylight, had been targeted north of the port of Khasab, causing four injuries amongst the Indian-Iranian crew present on the vessel. The Marshall Islands-flagged tanker MKD VYOM was damaged after being struck off the coast of Oman, killing one crew member. As a result of the conflict and higher risk to ships, 150 freight ships, including many oil tankers, stalled behind the strait.

target_text: thumb|An EAS warning sent out to residents in the UAE confirming safety.
description: thumb|An EAS warning sent out to residents in the UAE confirming safety.

target_text: thumb|Rescuers and residents searching through the rubble of the Minab girls' el
description: thumb|Rescuers and residents searching through the rubble of the Minab girls' elementary school, destroyed by the United StatesIsrael also conducted coordinated cyberattacks on Iranian infrastructure, media, and phone apps with messages calling on Iranians to rise up against their government. The popular BadeSaba Calendar prayer app, with over 5 million downloads, was compromised in the attack, being hacked early that morning to broadcast push notifications in Persian urging military personnel and citizens to defect, lay down weapons, and join opposition forces with messages such as "Help has arrived" and "It's time for reckoning."

target_text: thumb|left|Drone attacks and strikes in the UAE from 28 February to 10 March.
description: thumb|left|Drone attacks and strikes in the UAE from 28 February to 10 March.

description: | video1 = [https://www.youtube.com/shorts/MuHb2zLHh7o US consulate in Dubai hit by suspected Iranian drone]

target_text: * 20:38 UTC – US president Donald Trump gives the order to proceed with Operatio
description: * 20:38 UTC – US president Donald Trump gives the order to proceed with Operation Epic Fury.

target_text: 19 people were injured after an Iranian missile hit a neighborhood in Beersheba,
description: 19 people were injured after an Iranian missile hit a neighborhood in Beersheba, Israel.

target_text: Amazon AWS Bahrain Data Center (damaged by nearby drone strike)
description: An Iranian drone strike in close proximity to an Amazon Web Services (AWS) data center facility in Bahrain caused physical damage to the infrastructure on Monday March 2, 2026. AWS confirmed: "in Bahrain, a drone strike in close proximity to one of our facilities caused physical impact to our infrastructure." The facility was not directly struck (unlike the two UAE facilities — see s096) but sustained collateral structural and infrastructure damage from a nearby impact. Recovery expected to be "prolonged." AWS Bahrain region availability zones experienced outages alongside the UAE facilities. The Bahrain facility is part of the AWS Middle East (Bahrain) region (me-south-1).
source_urls: Reuters (March 2, 2026): "in Bahrain, a drone strike in close proximity to one of our facilities caused physical impact to our infrastructure" — AWS official status page statement cited by Reuters | Business Insider (March 2, 2026): "a third facility in Bahrain was damaged by a drone strike in close proximity" — citing AWS dashboard | The Register (March 2, 2026): AWS Middle East outages article confirming Bahrain facility

target_text: Amazon AWS UAE Data Centers (2 facilities directly struck)
description: Iranian drones directly struck two Amazon Web Services (AWS) data center facilities in the UAE on Monday March 2, 2026 — the first time a major US tech company's data centers have been disrupted by military action. AWS confirmed: "In the UAE, two of our facilities were directly struck." Structural damage was reported, power delivery disrupted, and fire suppression activities caused additional water damage. Recovery expected to be "prolonged given the nature of the physical damage involved." AWS UAE East region availability zones experienced outages. Financial institutions using AWS services were also affected. Previously on March 1, AWS had reported "objects" triggering a fire at a UAE data center; the March 2 statement confirmed direct drone strikes. AWS noted: "the broader operating environment in the Middle East remains unpredictable."
source_urls: Reuters (March 2, 2026): "In the UAE, two of our facilities were directly struck" — AWS official status page statement cited by Reuters | Business Insider (March 2, 2026): "Two facilities in the United Arab Emirates sustained direct hits" — citing AWS cloud service dashboard update | The Register (March 2, 2026): "AWS says drones hit two of its datacenters in UAE" — citing AWS status page | AWS official status page (March 2, 2026): "These strikes have caused structural damage, disrupted power delivery to our infrastructure, and in some cases required fire suppression activities that resulted in additional water damage"

target_text: Athe Nova — Honduras-Flagged Fuel/Asphalt Tanker
description: IRGC struck Honduras-flagged asphalt/fuel tanker Athe Nova in the Strait of Hormuz with two drones, leaving it ablaze on March 2, 2026. IRGC claimed the vessel was acting "in unison with America." Reuters confirmed the vessel identity. This is a distinct event from the earlier MV Skylight sinking (Musandam peninsula) and the Marshall Islands-flagged crude tanker off Muscat. Part of the ongoing IRGC maritime campaign targeting non-Iranian shipping in the Strait of Hormuz since the conflict began Feb 28.
source_urls: Reuters (March 2, 2026): "Iran's Revolutionary Guards said a fuel tanker, identified as the Honduran-flagged Athe Nova, was burning in the Strait of Hormuz after being hit by two drones, Iranian news agencies reported" | The Guardian (March 2, 2026): "Iranian forces claimed on Monday to have hit the Honduras-flagged fuel tanker Athe Nova in the strait with two drones, leaving it ablaze" | Al-Monitor (March 2, 2026, citing Reuters): confirmed Athe Nova name and drone strike in Strait of Hormuz

target_text: Baghdad International Airport — US Troop Positions
description: Iraqi Shiite militia Saraya Awliya al-Dam claimed a drone attack targeting US troops stationed at Baghdad International Airport — further widening the Iranian retaliation campaign following the killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei. Saraya Awliya al-Dam is a pro-Iran PMF-affiliated group that has previously targeted US forces in Iraq. The claim was widely reported via AP but US military confirmation of damage or casualties was not immediately available. The attack is part of a broader pattern of Iran-backed militia strikes on US bases across the region. Saraya Awliya al-Dam is listed by Wikipedia as a unit involved in this conflict.
source_urls: AP (Jon Gambrell, Melanie Lidman) | WRAL.com (AP wire) | San Diego Union-Tribune (AP wire) | Daily Mail | Times Now

target_text: Beirut — Israeli Airstrike Barrage (Hezbollah Retaliation)
description: Israel carried out heavy airstrikes on the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut (Dahiyeh) after Hezbollah launched missiles and drones toward Israel in retaliation for Khamenei's killing. More than a dozen explosions shook Beirut at ~3am local time Monday March 2. Lebanese security sources told Reuters that airstrikes hit three locations in Dahiyeh — Hezbollah's main stronghold. Buildings collapsed; streams of residents fled south Beirut by car and foot; highways gridlocked. Hezbollah said the attack was 'in retaliation for the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and continuous Israeli violations against Lebanon.' IDF chief of staff Eyal Zamir: 'Hezbollah opened a campaign against Israel overnight, and is fully responsible for any escalation.' Simultaneously, IDF also struck southern Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley (see s070). IDF issued evacuation orders for 55 villages/towns across Lebanon. First Israeli strikes on Beirut since the 2024 conflict. AT LEAST 10 KILLED per Reuters medical source (updated ~23:57 EST March 1).
source_urls: AP News (March 2, 2026 — 'Israel launches barrage of strikes on Lebanese capital') | Reuters (Lebanese security sources — 'Israel carried out heavy airstrikes on Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut') | The Guardian (March 2, 2026 — 'more than a dozen explosions shook Beirut') | Al Jazeera ('Israel bombs Beirut after Hezbollah launches rocket attack — March 2, 2026') | IDF (Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir statement) | Associated Press — evacuation orders for 50 Lebanese villages | Lebanon Health Ministry (31 killed, 149 wounded — Times of Israel liveblog, AFP, Guardian — March 2 2026)

target_text: Dubai International Airport
description: In the early hours of morning, Dubai International Airport was struck by a suspected air strike, leading to four staff being injured and an evacuation. The airport sustained "minor damage", and emergency teams were deployed immediately during evacuation. The incident was contained as contingency plans were already put in place. A fire also broke out at the Jebel Ali Port, which was attributed to by the Dubai government to debris from "aerial interception" of an Iranian attack. French naval air base Camp de la Paix near Zayed Port in Abu Dhabi was also struck by drones.

target_text: Hezbollah Positions — Southern Lebanon and Bekaa Valley
description: Israeli airstrikes expanded beyond Beirut's southern suburbs to strike Hezbollah positions across southern Lebanon (Nabatieh region) and the Bekaa Valley in eastern Lebanon simultaneously with the Beirut strikes (s068). Lebanese security sources told Reuters and the Guardian that strikes hit multiple areas of southern Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley. Separately, the IDF issued evacuation orders warning civilians in nearly 50 villages in eastern and southern Lebanon to evacuate their homes and move at least 1,000 meters to open areas — indicating a broad multi-front strike campaign across Lebanon rather than a single targeted strike. IDF Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir: 'Hezbollah opened a campaign against Israel overnight, and is fully responsible for any escalation.' Strikes are ongoing. This marks the broadest Israeli military action in Lebanon since the 2024 war and a significant expansion of the conflict.
source_urls: Reuters (Lebanese security sources) | The Guardian (Lebanese security sources) | AP News (IDF evacuation order)

target_text: Iranian Army positions — 5 soldiers killed, drone shot down over city
description: Strikes on Khorramabad in western Iran killed 5 Iranian army members per Tasnim news agency quoting an army statement. A drone was also shot down over the city. First confirmed strikes on this western city.
source_urls: Tasnim (army statement) | The Hindu | Gulf News | Times of Israel liveblog

target_text: Oil Tanker MKD VYOM — Unmanned Boat Attack off Muscat
description: An oil tanker (MKD VYOM) was attacked by an explosive-laden unmanned surface boat approximately 52 nautical miles off the coast of Muscat Governorate on Monday March 2. The attack caused a fire and explosion in the engine room, killing one crew member — an Indian national. The remaining 21 crew members were evacuated safely. Oman's Ministry of Defence confirmed the attack. Local media reported the victim was an Indian crew member. The vessel was struck by a remote-controlled boat — consistent with Iranian IRGC tactics previously deployed in the Gulf. Second confirmed maritime attack off Oman's coast in this conflict (after MV Skylight near Musandam).
source_urls: Oman Ministry of Defence (official statement) | PTI (Press Trust of India) | The Hindu (citing Oman MoD and PTI)

target_text: Paphos International Airport
description: In Cyprus, Paphos International Airport was evacuated upon the detection of a foreign unidentified object in the sky, and at the same time sirens sounded once again at Akrotiri base upon the detection of a possible threat. Later on the Cypriot government confirmed that the objects were two drones which were intercepted without causing any damages. After the incident, Greece announced that they would deploy frigates and F-16<nowiki/>s to defend Cyprus from any further strikes by Iran. Later in the day IRGC general Sardar Jabbari commented that there was a US presence on the island of Cyprus and that Iran planned to strike the island "with such intensity that the Americans will be forced to leave".

target_text: Qatar Power Plant (Mesaieed) and Ras Laffan Energy Facility
description: Qatar's defense ministry confirmed two Iranian drones hit energy infrastructure early Monday March 2: one struck a water tank at a power plant in Mesaieed Industrial City, the second hit an energy facility in Ras Laffan Industrial City (the world's largest LNG export hub). MAJOR UPDATE: QatarEnergy officially confirmed it has 'ceased production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) and associated products' following the attacks. QatarEnergy was also set to declare force majeure on LNG shipments (82% of customers are Asian). The shutdown of Ras Laffan sent European gas prices surging more than 50%. Bloomberg: 'Qatar shut down LNG production at the world's largest export facility after it was targeted in an Iranian drone attack, sending European gas prices surging more than 50% and rattling global energy markets.' Reuters confirmed QatarEnergy would declare force majeure. No casualties; Qatar hosts Al Udeid Air Base — the largest US air base in the Middle East — which Iran previously targeted with ballistic missiles and drones.
source_urls: Qatar Ministry of Defense (official statement) | The New York Times (liveblog — March 2, 2026)

target_text: Qatar shoots down two Iranian Su-24 bombers, making it the first nation to shoot
description: Qatar shoots down two Iranian Su-24 bombers, making it the first nation to shoot down manned Iranian aircraft in the conflict.

target_text: Road in West Jerusalem — Ballistic Missile Impact (March 2)
description: An Iranian ballistic missile struck a road in West Jerusalem on March 2, 2026, injuring several people. Al Jazeera video footage showed the missile bypassing Israel's Iron Dome and David's Sling air defense systems before impacting a road in the western part of the city. This is a distinct event from the March 1 Sultan's Pool impact near the Old City (which had no casualties). The exact injury count was described as 'several' by Al Jazeera. IDF had activated sirens across Jerusalem during multiple Iranian missile waves on Days 2-3 of the conflict. Ongoing IDF investigation into air defense failures during the Jerusalem impacts.
source_urls: Al Jazeera (video report — published March 2, 2026) | Times of Israel liveblog (March 1-2, 2026)

target_text: Saudi Aramco — Ras Tanura Oil Refinery
description: Iranian drones targeted Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura oil refinery on the Persian Gulf coast near Dammam early Monday March 2. UPDATED: Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry spokesperson Turki al-Maliki confirmed to Al Arabiya that BOTH DRONES WERE INTERCEPTED — the fire at the refinery was caused by falling debris from the interception, not a direct hit. Saudi energy ministry confirmed 'limited fire was under control and had no impact on oil supplies.' Refinery was shut down as a precaution; no civilian injuries confirmed. Online videos showed thick black smoke from the debris fire. Ras Tanura is one of the world's largest refineries (~550,000 bpd capacity). An analyst at Verisk Maplecroft called it 'a significant escalation, with Gulf energy infrastructure now squarely in Iran's sights' even with interception. Saudi Arabia had previously been targeted at Prince Sultan Air Base (s029) — this is the first Iranian drone attack targeting Saudi civilian energy infrastructure in this conflict.
source_urls: Saudi Arabia Defense Ministry (official statement via Saudi Press Agency) | Bloomberg (Aramco operations halted) | AP News (March 2, 2026) | NDTV | Times of Israel liveblog | Moneycontrol

target_text: Tehran
description: The Iranian Red Crescent reported 555 people killed. Israel launched strikes on targets in Tehran and Sanandaj, hitting military and strategic locations including command centers and missile sites. Subsequently, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) confirmed that satellite imagery showed that entrance buildings at Natanz were damaged, but no radiological consequence was expected and no additional impact had been detected at the fuel enrichment plant itself. The Khatam-al-Anbia and Gandhi hospitals in Tehran were hit by strikes. The USAF hit military aircraft and drone launchers, including multiple F-14 Tomcats at airbases across Iran. According to Al Arabiya, Israeli special forces and Mossad operatives carried out a ground operation inside Iran; no details of the operation were reported.

target_text: The Al Minhad Air Base
description: The Al Minhad Air Base was also attacked by Iran. The Air Base is operated by the United Arab Emirates Air Force and the Royal Air Force. It is also home to Camp Baird, the Australian Defence Force Headquarters Middle East (HQME).

target_text: The IRGC claimed a drone strike on the Honduras-flagged tanker Athe Nova in the
description: The IRGC claimed a drone strike on the Honduras-flagged tanker Athe Nova in the Strait of Hormuz.

target_text: The United States Embassy in Bahrain issued a warning to Americans in the countr
description: The United States Embassy in Bahrain issued a warning to Americans in the country to avoid hotels in the capital, Manama, following the incidents. The embassy reported that one of the struck buildings was the Crowne Plaza hotel.

target_text: Three US F-15 fighters crash in Kuwait. Iran claims to have shot them down, whil
description: Three US F-15 fighters crash in Kuwait. Iran claims to have shot them down, while later CentCom attributed it to friendly fire by Kuwait.

target_text: Two US Department of Defense employees were wounded by Iranian strikes.
description: Two US Department of Defense employees were wounded by Iranian strikes.

target_text: US Embassy Kuwait City compound — Iranian drone strike
description: An Iranian drone struck the US Embassy compound in Kuwait City on Monday March 2. The embassy announced Tuesday it had been closed until further notice. The US State Department ordered evacuation of non-emergency personnel and family members from Kuwait as a precaution. The strike followed Iran's ongoing barrage against Gulf states hosting US military assets. No confirmed casualties at the embassy compound. Separately, the US Embassy in Riyadh was also struck by two Iranian drones on the same night (see s088). Kuwait also hosted the US military aircraft friendly-fire incident (three F-15Es mistakenly shot down by Kuwaiti forces).
source_urls: AP News (March 3, 2026): 'It followed an attack on the U.S. Embassy in Kuwait, which announced Tuesday it had been closed until further notice.' | NYT live updates (March 3, 2026 3:46 AM ET): 'A drone also struck the American Embassy compound in Kuwait on Monday, according to two U.S. officials who spoke anonymously because they were not authorized to discuss the matter publicly.' | US State Department: embassy closed until further notice; non-emergency personnel ordered evacuated from Kuwait

target_text: US Embassy Kuwait City — Drone Strike
description: A drone struck the American embassy compound in Kuwait City on Monday March 2, confirmed by two US officials who spoke anonymously to the New York Times. NYT verified video showing smoke billowing from the area surrounding the embassy complex. Earlier, the US Embassy had warned personnel not to come to the building and to take cover, citing a 'continuing threat of missile and drone attacks over Kuwait.' The State Department was notified. The drone strike on the embassy followed Iranian attacks on US bases across Kuwait and the US military aircraft crashes west of Al Jahra. First confirmed strike directly on the US Embassy compound.
source_urls: Two US officials (via The New York Times — anonymously confirmed) | The New York Times (video of smoke from embassy complex verified by Times) | AFP (US Embassy statement — personnel warned not to come to embassy)

target_text: US Embassy — Riyadh Diplomatic Quarter
description: Two Iranian drones struck the US Embassy compound in Riyadh's Diplomatic Quarter, confirmed by Saudi Arabia's Defence Ministry. Saudi MoD stated a 'limited' fire broke out at the US Embassy compound with minor structural damage. Four AFP witnesses reported hearing two explosions and seeing thick smoke rising over the Diplomatic Quarter. A senior US official confirmed the embassy was hit, likely by a UAV (Axios/Barak Ravid). Reuters cited two sources confirming a fire at the embassy after an explosion. India Today confirmed: 'Saudi Arabia's Defence Ministry has confirmed that a limited fire broke out at the US Embassy in Riyadh after it was hit by two drones.' This is the FIRST confirmed physical drone strike to impact Saudi Arabian territory in this conflict — previous Iranian drone/missile attacks on Ras Tanura/Aramco were both intercepted before impact. Part of Iran's Day 3 campaign targeting US diplomatic and military facilities across the Gulf. No confirmed casualties as of initial reporting.
source_urls: Saudi Arabia Defence Ministry (official, via Al Arabiya — March 2 2026) | Reuters (two sources, via Ynet News — March 2 2026) | The Guardian liveblog (March 2 2026) | India Today (March 2 2026) | Times of India / AFP (four witnesses — March 2 2026) | Axios / Barak Ravid (senior US official confirmation — March 2 2026)

target_text: US Navy Fifth Fleet base
description: * US Navy Fifth Fleet base heavily damaged in Iranian attacks

target_text: West Jerusalem Road — Iranian Ballistic Missile Direct Hit
description: An Iranian ballistic missile struck a road in West Jerusalem early March 2, causing property damage to the surrounding road, damaging a civilian vehicle, and wounding six people. Al Jazeera published video footage of the aftermath. Wikipedia documents the strike as part of Iran's Day 3 missile campaign. Separate from the earlier warhead impact near Sultan's Pool (s055, Feb 28) and the Tel Aviv missile strike (s028). Part of Iran's continued Day 3 missile offensive after overnight lull.
source_urls: Wikipedia (2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran) | Al Jazeera (video — 'Iranian ballistic missile strike on West Jerusalem')

target_text: Yazd — 6 Military/IRGC Sites
description: Iran's state-run news agency IRNA reported early Monday March 2 that a series of US-Israeli strikes hit six locations around the central desert city of Yazd. IRNA warned locals 'to avoid unnecessary travel.' Yazd sits approximately 660 km (410 miles) southeast of Tehran in the central desert and is a major industrial city. The six strikes represent one of the broadest coordinated strike packages on a single city outside Tehran. IRCS confirmed 131 cities total under attack as of March 2 — Yazd's inclusion suggests the campaign continues to expand geographically beyond the previously confirmed target set. No specific targets named by IRNA; likely IRGC, air defense, or missile-related facilities given the strike density. First confirmed strikes on Yazd in this conflict.
source_urls: IRNA (Iranian state media) | AP News (March 2 2026 liveblog) | Times of Israel liveblog

target_text: an oil refinery
description: Iran launched more strikes against the Gulf States. Explosions were reported in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha. Smoke rose from the US embassy in Kuwait, According to Kuwait News Agency, an oil refinery was hit by shrapnel. US-flagged tanker Stena Imperative was hit by projectiles off Bahrain, causing a fire that was extinguished. In Bahrain, sirens had sounded across the country. An Iranian strike targeted Mina Salman port, causing a fire. UAE officials claimed to have downed 148 Iranian drones, nine ballistic missiles, and half a dozen cruise missiles.

target_text: as the Hamad International Airport
description: On 2 March 2026, Qatari official Majed al-Ansari stated that a new wave of Iranian missiles that targeted locations such as the Hamad International Airport were intercepted, while announcing that Qatar would not engage with Iran. The same day, the Ministry of Defence in Qatar claimed that Qatari F-15<nowiki/>s had downed two Su-24 bombers from Iran and intercepted multiple drone and missile strikes. Anonymous sources told CNN that the Su-24 targeted al-Udeid Air Base and Ras Laffan Industrial City, and that the aircraft were two minutes away from reaching Qatari territory when they were downed. A search is underway to recover the crew. This was the first air-air kill for the QEAF. A UK Royal Air Force Typhoon shot down an attack drone directed at Qatar. That same day, the Qatari Ministry of Defense announced that Ras Laffan Industrial City and Mesaieed Industrial Area were struck by two Iranian drones.

target_text: it attacked an IDF base
description: Projectiles were launched from within Lebanon toward Israel in the early hours of 2 March, setting off sirens across Haifa and the Upper Galilee. The IDF said that it intercepted a rocket from Hezbollah, while others hit open areas. The IDF spokesperson issued an emergency statement saying that the attack is to be considered "an official declaration of war by Hezbollah", vowing to "neutralize" the threat. Hezbollah confirmed its responsibility, saying that it attacked an IDF base in Haifa in response to Khamenei's killing.

target_text: occurred near the Erbil Airport
description: An explosion occurred near the Erbil Airport after an attack by Iraqi militia drones.

target_text: refining facility
description: A refining facility was damaged in Aramco's Ras Tanura in Saudi Arabia, causing a contained fire. It was initially believed to be caused by a drone attack, but Saudi officials stated it was debris from an intercepted Iranian missile, and Iran denied it had targeting the facility. All staff members at the US-Jordanian embassy were evacuated "due to a threat". Two drones targeted the US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport, one of which reportedly struck the base. There were reports of explosions in Erbil, Kurdistan Region, Iraq. A pro-Iran group called the "Guardians of the Blood Brigade" stated they were behind these two attacks, and claimed they were in retaliation for Khamenei's assassination.

target_text: the base
description: Around 00:20 UTC, the Royal Air Force station at Akrotiri, Cyprus, was targeted by a suspected drone strike, setting off air raid sirens across Akrotiri and Dhekelia. Five drones are reportedly intercepted although one managed to hit the airfield, causing minor damage. The British military automated alert system stated that there was an "ongoing security threat", and the UK Ministry of Defence claimed that "the base has responded" to the attack. This was later confirmed by Cypriot authorities. The drones were launched before the UK allowed the US to use its military bases for operations against Iran. UK Defence Secretary John Healey said that he did not believe they were under attack. Defence Minister of Cyprus Vasilis Palmas denied that missiles were launched at Cyprus, and Prime Minister Keir Starmer affirmed this.

target_text: the direction of the base
description: The American Fifth Fleet headquarters, located in Bahrain, was struck during an Iranian missile and drone attack on 28 February. Around 300 British personnel were stationed nearby, but no casualties were reported. Later footage showed smoke rising in the direction of the base. CBC News, citing the Canadian Department of National Defence's website, reported that Canadian soldiers were present on exchange with the United States military at the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain at the time of the attack.
