target_text: According to sources in Bahrain, a warehouse caught fire due to shrapnel after a
description: According to sources in Bahrain, a warehouse caught fire due to shrapnel after another Iranian attack.

target_text: Assembly of Experts Building (Qom)
description: IDF struck the Assembly of Experts building in Qom — the second Assembly of Experts facility struck in this conflict (s102 was the Tehran building, earlier on March 3). Iranian media reported US and Israeli strikes hit Assembly buildings in both Qom and Tehran. The Qom Assembly premises were evacuated before the strike; no casualties reported. The Assembly of Experts is the 88-member clerical body constitutionally responsible for electing Iran's next supreme leader. The timing coincides with the Assembly's virtual session to choose a Khamenei successor — likely intended to disrupt or intimidate the succession process. Reuters: "The building housing Iran's Assembly of Experts, tasked with choosing a leader to replace Khamenei, was also flattened by an air attack in the city of Qom, Iranian news agencies said. Israel said it was still reviewing the results of that strike."
source_urls: Iran International (opposition channel) | IranIntl.com liveblog (March 3 2026) | Reuters (March 3 2026) | Fars News Agency (semiofficial Iranian)

target_text: Bahrain reported that the industrial area of Ma'ameer was targeted by "Iranian a
description: Bahrain reported that the industrial area of Ma'ameer was targeted by "Iranian aggression" causing "limited material damage" and no casualties.

target_text: Bahrain said that it had intercepted 125 missiles and 211 drones from Iran since
description: Bahrain said that it had intercepted 125 missiles and 211 drones from Iran since the beginning of the war.

target_text: Bahrain said that it intercepted Iranian missiles and drones overnight.
description: Bahrain said that it intercepted Iranian missiles and drones overnight.

target_text: By the beginning of March, the Bahraini government said it had intercepted 54 dr
description: By the beginning of March, the Bahraini government said it had intercepted 54 drone and missile strikes across the country.

target_text: CENTCOM Day 4 wave — 'hitting Iran surgically, overwhelmingly, and unapologetically'
description: US Central Command announced a new wave of strikes early March 3 (local time): 'This morning, US forces are hitting Iran surgically, overwhelmingly, and unapologetically.' Day 4 of operations.
source_urls: CENTCOM (official X post) | NYT liveblog | Al Jazeera

target_text: Debris from an Iranian drone attack on an office tower in the Seef District kill
description: Debris from an Iranian drone attack on an office tower in the Seef District killed a 29-year old Bahraini woman and injured eight others.

target_text: Fujairah
description: Ship refueling slowed at Fujairah after debris from a drone interception fell in the area of the Fujairah Free Zone.

target_text: Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (oil storage tanks)
description: Iranian drone was intercepted over Fujairah (UAE's eastern coast oil hub), but shrapnel/debris fell on the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone, causing a fire at oil storage facilities. The Fujairah Government Media Office confirmed the interception and that debris landed in the zone, causing a fire that was subsequently extinguished and operations resumed. Argus Media confirmed storage operators including Vopak, VTTI, MENA and GPS had already suspended operations and restricted staff access before the strike. Bunkering operations at the port were not suspended. Hindustan Times published photographs showing thick black smoke rising above the Fujairah industrial zone. No casualties reported. Fujairah is critically significant as it lies outside the Strait of Hormuz — it is a key Hormuz bypass hub that Iran appears to be targeting to cut off alternative oil routing. First confirmed Iranian strike on Fujairah's oil infrastructure in the conflict.
source_urls: Fujairah Government Media Office (official statement via Al Jazeera liveblog — March 3, 2026) | Argus Media (argusmedia.com — March 3, 2026: 'UAE's Fujairah storage tanks hit by drones'; confirmed fire and storage operator suspensions) | Arab News (March 3, 2026: 'Drone debris causes fire at oil industry site in UAE's city of Fujairah, operations resume: media office')

target_text: Hezbollah Command Centers and Weapons Storage — IDF Day 4 Wave
description: IDF Day 4 strike wave on Dahiyeh (southern Beirut suburbs) — Israeli military confirmed it was attacking Hezbollah command centers and weapons storage facilities in Beirut early Tuesday March 3. Part of IDF Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir's declared "offensive campaign" against Hezbollah following the group's opening of the Lebanon front on March 2. IDF simultaneously struck Iran and Lebanon. Hezbollah's leader Naim Qassem declared a "marked target for elimination" by Israeli Defense Minister Katz. Lebanon Health Ministry toll stands at 52 killed, 154 wounded from all Lebanon strikes since Day 2.
source_urls: NYT live updates (March 3 2026, 1:02 AM ET update): "the Israeli military said it was attacking again in Iran and in Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah command centers and weapons storage facilities in the capital, Beirut" | IDF official statement (cited by NYT, March 3 2026) — Day 4 offensive campaign against Hezbollah, declared by IDF Chief of Staff Zamir as part of multi-wave operation

target_text: Hotel housing US troops — Erbil city center
description: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq launched a drone swarm attack on a hotel in Erbil housing US troops — early hours of March 3, 2026 (local time). The Iran-aligned faction Saraya Awliya Al Dam also claimed involvement. Video from Iraqi Naya news agency showed a building engulfed in flames. This is the first attack by Iran-aligned Iraqi militant groups on US positions in the Kurdistan Region since the start of the Iran conflict. Al Jazeera and Reuters confirmed the strike. No US casualty figures released; US forces had an active presence in Erbil under a continuing agreement through 2026.
source_urls: Reuters (via ABC Australia: "The Islamic Resistance in Iraq says it has carried out a drone swarm attack on a hotel housing US troops in Erbil" — March 3, 2026) | Al Jazeera (via Wikipedia 2026 Iran conflict, 3 March section — March 3, 2026) | The National News ("An Iran-backed faction, Saraya Awliya Al Dam, used drones to strike US forces in Erbil" — March 3, 2026) | Times Now / Moneycontrol (video from Iraqi Naya news agency showing building engulfed in flames in Erbil)

target_text: In late February 2026, the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran to
description: In late February 2026, the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran to target strategic facilities and core government officials, starting a conflict that is currently ongoing. The airstrikes were announced by United States President Donald Trump shortly after, who stated that the operation was intended to impair the Iranian nuclear program and initiate a regime change following unsuccessful talks immediately prior. Iran retaliated shortly after, striking nations across the Persian Gulf, including Qatar and its close neighbors Bahrain, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

target_text: Iran banned food exports, and ordered people to uninstall and disable foreign so
description: Iran banned food exports, and ordered people to uninstall and disable foreign social media apps on their phones over security concerns. State media announced that Iran has stocked one month of wheat flour and essential food products. Iran announced that the IRGC was setting up checkpoints in cities nationwide. State media reported that Ali Khamenei's son and possible heir Mojtaba Khamenei "miraculously survived missile strike" that ended his father's life. He was subsequently elected as the new Supreme Leader of Iran.

target_text: Iran continued its strikes on Israel and the Gulf states.
description: Iran continued its strikes on Israel and the Gulf states.

target_text: Iranian military installation — Kerman Province
description: US-Israeli airstrikes killed 13 Iranian troops in Kerman, approximately 800km (500 miles) southeast of Tehran, according to semiofficial Tasnim news agency cited by AP. Kerman is the capital of Kerman Province in southeastern Iran and marks one of the southernmost confirmed strike locations in the campaign. The strikes represent a geographic expansion of the US-Israel operation further into southeastern Iran. Kerman Province borders Hormozgan Province (where Minab school strike occurred) to the south. This is the first confirmed strike on Kerman in the current conflict, extending the confirmed list of Iranian cities struck to 15.
source_urls: Tasnim News Agency (semiofficial Iranian news outlet) — cited in AP News March 3, 2026 | AP News (March 3, 2026): 'The semiofficial Tasnim news agency reported airstrikes killed 13 Iranian troops in Kerman, 800 kilometers (500 miles) southeast of Iran\'s capital, Tehran.'

target_text: Isfahan
description: Isfahan, Kermanshah, Shiraz and IEI were bombed, along with other cities. IRIB Tehran HQ was bombed second day in row and switched to Telewebion IP internet streaming TV. Old Mirdamad St., the historic imperial Senate of Iran and the current seat of the Assembly of Experts was bombed, as was the Expediency Discernment Council building. The IDF said that it destroyed a covert nuclear site, as well as the commander of the Quds Force's Lebanon branch, in Tehran. The IDF said it had destroyed 300 Iranian missile launchers since the war began. The IDF announced that it had destroyed the Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) headquarters, along with command-and-control infrastructure and the president's office, noting that it targeted "the regime's most central and significant headquarters". Golestan Palace, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was hit by debris from an Israeli-US airstrike, causing some damage including to the Brilliant Hall and the Marble Throne. The Ministry of Cultural Heritage, Tourism and Handicrafts officially asked UNESCO to evaluate the situation, and UNESCO issued a statement, noting that damaging UNESCO property is against international laws. The Iranian Red Crescent Society raised the number killed to 787.

target_text: Mina Salman Port
description: At approximately 2 AM, Mina Salman Port in Bahrain was struck by an Iranian missile, and a Bangladeshi shipyard worker was killed by falling debris after another Iranian missile was intercepted there. The strike injured 2 more and set the American MT Stena Imperative oil tanker ablaze. The crew aboard the ship reported no casualties. Additionally, debris from an impact that day hit an Amazon Web Services data center, causing electricity issues there.

target_text: Officials reported that three buildings in Manama and Muharraq were subject to d
description: Officials reported that three buildings in Manama and Muharraq were subject to drone attacks and falling debris from an intercepted missile, with footage showing a drone strike the Era Views Tower. Air raid sirens were activated following the attack.

target_text: On 11 March, Qatar said that they intercepted Iranian missiles and drones overni
description: On 11 March, Qatar said that they intercepted Iranian missiles and drones overnight.

target_text: On 2 March, UAE Defence Ministry reported the extent of its operations facing Ir
description: On 2 March, UAE Defence Ministry reported the extent of its operations facing Iran. The Ministry updated its interception figure to 174 ballistic missiles tracked (161 intercepted and 13 falling to the sea) and 689 detected drones (645 intercepted) plus eight cruise missiles which struck the country. Educational institutions shifted to remote learning.

target_text: On 3 March 2026, Channel 12 (Israel) and various western media outlets reported
description: On 3 March 2026, Channel 12 (Israel) and various western media outlets reported that Qatar participated in strikes on Iran following Iranian attempts to directly strike Doha's airport. Majed al-Ansari denied that Qatar had joined the "campaign targeting Iran".

target_text: Port of Duqm — fuel tank struck by drone
description: A second Iranian drone attack on Oman's Port of Duqm occurred on Day 4 of the conflict. Several drones targeted the port; one struck and hit a fuel tank. No casualties reported. Oman's state news agency (ONA) confirmed the strike. The first Duqm attack (Day 2) wounded 1 port worker (see s035); this second strike directly hit infrastructure but caused no injuries. Duqm is a major commercial port and industrial zone on Oman's Arabian Sea coast, approximately 550km south of Muscat. Oman had previously served as a neutral mediator in Iran-US talks, making these repeated strikes on Omani territory diplomatically significant.
source_urls: Oman News Agency (ONA) — Oman state news agency, cited in Guardian liveblog 03:59 EST March 3, 2026: "The Port of Duqm in Oman was targeted by several drones, one of which struck a fuel tank, according to the country's state news agency." | The Guardian liveblog (March 3, 2026 ~04:02 EST): "State media: Oman port targeted in suspected drone strike" — no casualties confirmed

target_text: Ramat David Air Base — Hezbollah Drone Attack
description: Hezbollah announced a drone attack on Ramat David Air Base in northern Israel's Jezreel Valley — part of Day 4 continued operations by the Lebanon-based group. Ramat David is one of Israel's largest northern air force bases, housing F-16 squadrons. Consistent with Hezbollah's declared campaign against Israeli military targets following Khamenei's killing. Israel simultaneously launched new airstrikes on southern Beirut (Dahiyeh) and issued new evacuation orders for dozens of locations in Lebanon including two southern Beirut neighbourhoods. Lebanon's government separately banned Hezbollah military activities (Reuters, March 2) but the group continued operations. Confidence medium — Hezbollah claim confirmed by Guardian but awaiting IDF damage assessment.
source_urls: Hezbollah official statement (via Pravda EN, Kurdistan24 — March 3, 2026) | The Guardian liveblog (March 3, 2026 — 'Hezbollah said it had launched drones at northern Israel')

target_text: Security Headquarters (area housing US personnel)
description: A projectile struck Sulaymaniyah in Iraqi Kurdistan on Tuesday night, triggering a massive fire on a hill allegedly housing a security force headquarters. According to a security official cited by Rudaw, the targeted site is an area where American personnel are stationed. Videos show flames rising from the scene; at least four ambulances responded. Attack is attributed to Iran or Iran-aligned militias targeting US presence in the Kurdistan Region of Iraq. This is distinct from the Erbil hotel drone attack (s092) earlier in the conflict. Confidence MEDIUM — awaiting second-source confirmation on Iran attribution and US presence detail.
source_urls: Rudaw (Kurdish media outlet) | Times Now liveblog (March 4 2026 IST — March 3 2026 ET)

target_text: Sheikh Isa Air Base — IRGC Wave 14 Drone & Missile Attack
description: IRGC launched 'Wave 14 of Operation Sincere Promise 4' — a large-scale drone and missile attack on Sheikh Isa Air Base, the primary US Air Force base in Bahrain (Sakhir). The IRGC used 20 UAVs and 3 ballistic missiles. IRGC claims destruction of the 'main command building and headquarters' and fuel tanks set on fire. Confirmed by IRNA and multiple independent outlets. Sheikh Isa Air Base is separate from the Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Manama (s033). It hosts US Air Force assets and is a key hub for US air operations in the Gulf. IRGC 'destruction' claims unverified by US CENTCOM as of reporting.
source_urls: IRNA (Iranian state news agency, official IRGC statement — March 3, 2026) | The Guardian liveblog (March 3, 2026 — Day 4 opening summary) | Kurdistan24 (March 3, 2026) | Times of India liveblog (March 3, 2026) | Pravda USA (March 3, 2026 — citing IRGC statement via IRNA)

target_text: Tehran
description: A joint US-Israeli strike targeted Azadi square in Tehran as well as military facilities, and the metro station. The IAF also struck an Iranian military complex in eastern Tehran. Israeli strikes hit the Basij headquarters, as well as missile launch pads, defense systems and the supply and logistics directorate affiliated with the regime's ground forces. At 16:15 UTC Israel announced it had completed a major large scale airstrikes on a major military outpost housing soldiers from all branches of Iran's security apparatus.

target_text: The Israeli cities of Bnei Brak and Rosh HaAyin saw Iranian strikes causing a to
description: The Israeli cities of Bnei Brak and Rosh HaAyin saw Iranian strikes causing a total of seven injuries amongst civilians. Israeli Police report that one person was injured after Iranian shrapnel fell in Tel Aviv.

target_text: The US embassy in Riyadh was struck by two Iranian drones, causing a fire and pr
description: The US embassy in Riyadh was struck by two Iranian drones, causing a fire and prompting its closure. Iran launched strikes against Irbil, Iraq. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq also claimed responsibility for explosions in Erbil.

target_text: US Consulate Complex — Dubai
description: Iranian drone struck the US Consulate complex in Dubai. Videos shared on social media and verified by The New York Times show a large fire at the US Consulate complex. A loud buzzing sound consistent with Iranian Shaheed drones was heard immediately before an explosion in verified clips. The Dubai media office confirmed the fire was contained and that no injuries were reported. It is unclear how much structural damage the consulate sustained or which specific buildings were hit. Second US diplomatic facility struck in the Gulf after the Riyadh Embassy (s088) on March 2. UAE has now seen 5 deaths and 58 injured from Iranian strikes overall.
source_urls: NYT (verified video — Aric Toler, Ainara Tiefenthaler, Dmitriy Khavin, March 3 2026) | Dubai Media Office (official — fire contained, no injuries) | NYT live updates (March 3, 2026 ~3:00 PM ET)

target_text: US base
description: During the Twelve-Day War in June 2025, Iran struck a US base in the country. Iranian officials reportedly gave Qatar advance notice of the attack before launching it, which was intended to minimize damage and allow for de-escalation.

target_text: Western diplomats and other sources told The Jerusalem Post that Qatar had struc
description: Western diplomats and other sources told The Jerusalem Post that Qatar had struck Iran after Iranian attempts to directly strike Doha's airport and Qatar shooting down 2 Iranian Su-24 bombers; Channel 12 also reported Qatari strikes. Qatari Foreign Ministry spokesperson Majed al-Ansari vigorously denied the accusation that Qatar had joined the "campaign targeting Iran". According to Tasnim, GCC Gulf Shield units were deployed to Bahrain to quell Shia protests.

target_text: attack on Hamad International Airport
description: In the early hours of the day, sirens were activated in Bahrain as the government called for residents to stay safe. Amazon Web Services were targeted across the Gulf states causing significant damages in their facilities located in the UAE and Bahrain. The UAE detected numerous missiles heading from Iran 3:30 a.m. local time (23:30 GMT). The US Consulate in Al Seef, Dubai was struck by multiple drones, resulting in a fire. An attack on Hamad International Airport was stopped by Qatari forces.

target_text: close to the airport
description: An Iranian strike on a fuel depot in the Muharraq Governorate, close to the airport, resulted in a large fire. The Ministry of Interior announced the arrest of 4 Bahraini nationals for spying and transmitting sensitive information to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps . The Bahrain Defence Force announced that it had intercepted 112 ballistic missiles and 186 drones since the start of the war.

target_text: desalination plant
description: An Iranian drone damaged a desalination plant in Bahrain.

target_text: in the first land base
description: At 10:00 UTC Syrian forces blocked the Syrian - Lebanese border, in what they said was an effort to block Hezbollah from crossing the border and smuggling weapons in and out of Lebanon. At 11:55 to 12:35 UTC Multiple rockets, missiles, and drones launched towards Israel from Lebanon to the north and Iran from the east, and at 12:35 UTC Israel launched strikes at Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon.Israeli ground forces moved into Lebanon skirmishing with Hezbollah in the first land based clashes of the war. Hezbollah said that it clashed with Israeli forces attempting to advance in Khiam,and launched rocket barrages at a concentration of troops in Kiryat Shmona. Hezbollah also said that it struck an Israeli tank in the town of Houla, Lebanon. Israel later said that two of its soldiers were injured by anti-tank fire.

target_text: jets to protect its base
description: France deployed Rafale jets to protect its bases following the attacks at Camp de la Paix and Al Dhafra. An Iranian drone strike on the United States consulate in Dubai caused a fire to break out. The government's media office claimed to have contained it with no injuries.

target_text: massive natural gas facility
description: On 18 March, Iran launched strikes targeting and hitting Qatar's massive natural gas facility. Following this attack, Qatar ordered Iran's military and security attaches, along with their staff, to leave the country within 24 hours.

target_text: petroleum refinery
description: An explosion near Manama sparked a fire near a petroleum refinery and resulted in injuries to at least 32 Bahraini citizens, four of them "serious cases". An analysis by academic researchers examined by Reuters found that an American-operated Patriot missile was likely involved in the blast after it downed an Iranian drone mid-air. Following the attack on its oil refinery complex, Bahrain’s state oil company BAPCO announced force majeure on its group ​operations.

target_text: struck Al Udeid Air Base
description: On 3 March, an Iranian missile struck Al Udeid Air Base, but did not cause any casualties.

target_text: trio of Israeli base
description: Israel's Defense Minister authorized a ground invasion of Lebanon, to seize strategic positions. Israel said it detained a dozen Hezbollah members after a rocket strike on Israel. Hezbollah also launched drones and missiles at a trio of Israeli bases, andreportedly struck down an Israeli drone in Nabatieh. World Health Organization Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said that three paramedics were killed and six injured while working to help affected people in southern Lebanon.

target_text: According to an unnamed official from the Coalition of Political Forces of Irani
description: According to an unnamed official from the Coalition of Political Forces of Iranian Kurdistan, Iraqi-based Kurdish armed groups consisting of thousands of the Kurdistan Free Life Party (PJAK) troops began a military offensive into Iranian territory, with operations beginning earlier on 2 March. American and Israeli officials reportedly confirmed the events. However, an unnamed PJAK official denied the claims of an ongoing offensive and cross-border troop movements, as did Aziz Ahmed, deputy chief of staff to Kurdistan Region's prime minister Masrour Barzani.

target_text: At approximately near midnight, explosions were heard in a US consulate. Authori
description: At approximately near midnight, explosions were heard in a US consulate. Authorities said that a limited fire broke out in the consulate following a drone targeting operation, and was quickly contained with no injuries.

target_text: Basij Paramilitary HQ + Internal Security Force Facilities
description: IDF launched attacks on dozens of targets belonging to the Basij — Iran's state-run paramilitary militia — and the country's internal security forces in the early hours of Day 5 (March 4, 2026). The Basij (Mobilization Resistance Force), a IRGC-subordinate paramilitary, played a central role in the deadly crackdown on Iran's recent antigovernment protests. IDF statement: it had "launched attacks on dozens of targets belonging to the Basij, a state-run Iranian paramilitary force, and the country's internal security forces." This represents Israel's first explicit targeting announcement of the Basij as an institution in the current conflict — shifting focus from leadership decapitation and nuclear infrastructure toward the internal suppression apparatus. Broad wave across multiple Basij locations. Casualties unknown as of reporting.
source_urls: IDF official statement (via NYT live blog — March 4 2026, Aaron Boxerman) | Reuters (March 4 2026 — U.S. and Israel continued 24/7 bombardment, IDF broad wave)

target_text: Bushehr
description: In southern Iran, Bushehr Airport was targeted in a precision strike that resulted in the total destruction of an Iran Air Airbus A319 (EP-IEP) parked on the tarmac. The Mehr News Agency reported significant structural damage to the airport terminal. The proximity of the strikes to the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant (About away) prompted the Russian agency Rosatom to suspend construction on new units and evacuate non-essential staff due to a loss of communication with Iranian officials.

target_text: CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper said that the United States "destroyed 17
description: CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper said that the United States "destroyed 17 Iranian ships, including the most operational Iranian submarine that now has a hole in its side." The U.S. sunk missile corvette IRIS Shahid Soleimani, the lead ship of an Iranian class with the same name. The U.S. sunk the submarine IRIS Fateh, Iran's only operational Fateh-class submarine. It was the first submarine to be sunk during a conflict since the Falklands War.

target_text: Civilian Tanker — ~30nm SE of Kuwait
description: Tanker at anchor approximately 30 nautical miles southeast of Kuwait struck by unknown projectile. Large explosion on port side, small craft seen leaving scene. Oil leaking from cargo tank — potential environmental impact. Vessel taking on water but crew safe. UKMTO investigating. Consistent with IRGC fast-boat/drone boat maritime harassment pattern. Attribution to Iran pending official investigation.

target_text: Fujairah
description: Debris from drone interceptions rained down on ships off the coast of Fujairah, causing damage.

target_text: Government Complex — Presidential Office + Security Sites (broad Day 5 wave)
description: Israeli Air Force launched a new 'broad wave of strikes' against government targets in Tehran on Day 5 (March 4, 2026), with the IDF specifically citing strikes on 'Iranian terror regime targets' including the presidential office. Heavy explosions were heard in east Tehran at approximately 08:00 UTC. This wave is distinct from the earlier Day 5 Basij/security forces wave (s112, ~00:32 UTC) and coincides with the Israeli F-35 air-to-air kill of the Iranian YAK-130 (s113, 08:40 UTC). The presidential office had been previously struck in s101 (Day 4 SNSC/leadership complex). A new wave specifically targeting 'government regime sites' represents an escalation in targeting civilian administrative infrastructure. IDF statement cited by Guardian: 'Israel has begun a "broad wave of attacks" across Iran targeting missile launch sites, air defence systems and other infrastructure linked to the Iranian "terror regime."' No confirmed casualty count from this wave at time of reporting.
source_urls: IDF official statement (via The Guardian Day 5 liveblog — March 4 2026, summary at 04:44 EST: 'Israel said it had launched a "broad wave of strikes" against government targets in Tehran, including the presidential office') | Hindustan Times Day 5 liveblog (March 4 2026 — '1 Hr 33 Mins ago: Israel launches broad-scale strikes in Tehran' relative to 03:14 PM IST / 09:44 UTC)

target_text: Hotel in Hazmieh
description: Israeli airstrike struck a hotel in Hazmieh, approximately 5km southeast of downtown Beirut. Lebanon's National News Agency (NNA) reported ambulances were dispatched to the scene. The Associated Press confirmed the strike. Hazmieh is a residential and commercial suburb east of Beirut. No casualty count confirmed yet. Strike reported in Mirror liveblog (04:45 UTC March 4, 2026). Part of Day 5 IDF operations against targets in and around Beirut following Hezbollah's continued engagement.

target_text: Iranian Navy Frigate IRIS Dena (Moudge-class) — SUNK
description: A submarine attack sank the Iranian Navy frigate IRIS Dena off the coast of Galle, southern Sri Lanka on Day 5. The Moudge-class frigate (capacity ~180 crew) signaled distress off Galle at approximately 06:00 local time (00:30 UTC); Sri Lanka responded. Reuters (Sri Lankan navy and defence ministry sources): "101 people missing, 1 dead, 78 injured" — vessel confirmed sunk. 79 people were rescued and brought to hospital in Galle (1 subsequently died, 1 seriously injured). The attacker is officially UNKNOWN — no nation has claimed responsibility. US and Israeli submarines have been operating in the region; CENTCOM Admiral Cooper had earlier stated the US was sinking "all of the Iranian navy" and that 17 ships had already been destroyed. The Iris Dena is a sister ship of the Jamaran (previously sunk at Chabahar pier, s070). This attack marks the first confirmed submarine attack of the conflict, and extends naval combat to the Indian Ocean — approximately 2,000 miles from the Strait of Hormuz. A significant escalation: Iranian naval vessels previously confirmed destroyed by CENTCOM operations were in the Arabian Gulf/Gulf of Oman. 101 missing from a ~180-crew vessel represents potentially catastrophic naval losses.

target_text: Israeli airstrike in the port
description: A U.S. submarine sunk the Iranian warship IRIS Dena off the coast of Galle, Sri Lanka, killing 87 of its crewmen. The Iranian frigate was returning from International Fleet Review 2026, which was held in Visakhapatnam, India. 32 of its crewmen were rescued by the Sri Lankan navy. At 13:15 UTC an Iranian warship sunk by U.S.-Israeli airstrike in the port of Bandar Abbas. The warship was a Soleimani Class Corvette. It was the second Iranian warship sunk that day. According to CENTCOM, the US military cumulatively destroyed 19 Iranian ships, 1 submarine, and nearly 2,000 targets by this point in the war.

target_text: Larnaca International Airport
description: Greek F-16 fighters downed multiple Iranian Shahed drones near Cyprus. Larnaca International Airport was temporarily closed in response. Reports suggested a closure of Cypriot airspace, but it was later dismissed as false by the Cypriot government. A ballistic missile was intercepted by NATO forces in Turkey in Dörtyol, Hatay Province, with a statement after interception stating that Turkey reserved the right to defend its territory and that the ordnance had fallen in its territory. No casualties were reported by the statement. A senior U.S. military official said that it had been shot down by an SM-3 interceptor launched from . An anonymous Turkish official stated that the missile was directed towards "Greek Cyprus". Non-essential American workers in Cyprus were asked to evacuate the country due to the risks of a possible Iranian incursion in the future. After, the US State Department issued a travel advisory for Cyprus during the current crisis. In an emergency meeting in Rome, Giorgia Meloni and other Italian officials considered sending reinforcement to Cyprus after the deployment by other European nations (Greece, France, Germany and the United Kingdom), specifically considering the deployment of the destroyer Caio Duilio as a possibility (with capability up to 350 kilometers).

target_text: Near Qamishli International Airport (stray Iranian missile)

Qamishli is located in the Hasakah Governorate of northeastern Syria near the Turkey-Iraq-Syria tripoint. Turkey separately confirmed an Iranian missile passed over Iraq and Syria before being intercepted by NATO over the Mediterranean. The Qamishli landing may be a separate Iranian missile that overflew or misfired into Syrian territory. No casualties reported. First known Iranian ordnance to land on Syrian soil in this conflict.

target_text: Safeen Prestige — Malta-flagged container ship hit by projectile, crew abandoned ship

IRGC had declared the Strait closed on Feb 28; this attack demonstrates continued Iranian interdiction of civilian shipping despite CENTCOM claims of having eliminated Iran’s surface naval presence. Attribution to Iran/IRGC consistent with conflict pattern. No crew casualties confirmed as of reporting.

target_text: Hezbollah stronghold — southern suburbs of Beirut (Dahiyeh) — major evening bombardment wave
description: Israel launched a major bombardment of Dahiyeh (Beirut southern suburbs) starting just after midnight Friday Beirut local time (22:00 UTC Thursday March 5 = 5 PM ET). The bombardment is the largest Israeli assault on Dahiyeh since the November 2024 ceasefire, and represents a significant escalation from targeted infrastructure strikes to broad-area bombardment of the densely populated Hezbollah stronghold. Lebanon Health Ministry updated cumulative toll to 123 killed, 683 injured across all Israeli strikes since Monday March 2. IDF had struck 80 Hezbollah targets in Lebanon in the prior 24 hours per IDF official statement. IDF forces acknowledged moving deeper into Lebanon than previously disclosed. The Komala/Sulaymaniyah strike is a separate strike from the Day 6 morning IRNA-confirmed 3-missile strike on Kurdish groups HQ logged earlier. The broader context: Iran is systematically targeting Kurdish opposition forces that it believes are being armed by the US and preparing ground incursions into northwestern Iran. target_text: One drone hit the terminal
description: Iranian drones entered Azerbaijani airspace. One drone hit the terminal of the Nakhchivan International Airport and another fell near a school in the village of Shakarabad. Azerbaijan summoned their Iranian ambassador, withdrew their diplomats from Iran, and promised a military response. Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi denied that Iran had attacked Azerbaijan, and suggested that it was an Israeli false flag attack. Azer News reported that a Telegram channel affiliated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, "Sepah Pasdaran News Channel", claimed responsibility for the attack in Nakhchivan. Azerbaijani president Ilham Aliyev said that "Iran committed terror acts against Azerbaijan". Turkey likewise condemned the strikes in Azerbaijan.

target_text: RAF Akrotiri — UK Sovereign Base Area
description: An Iranian-aligned drone launched from pro-Iranian militia in Lebanon or western Iraq struck RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus on Day 6 (March 5, 2026), hitting the UK Sovereign Base Area and prompting fury from local Cypriot ministers. UK Defence Secretary John Healey flew to Cyprus to address the diplomatic fallout. The drone evaded detection by flying at low altitude and slow speed. RAF Akrotiri hosts British F-35B and Typhoon jets that the UK had been using for defensive missions under US-authorized basing agreements. It also serves as a major US and UK intelligence-gathering hub in the Eastern Mediterranean. This is the first confirmed strike on a NATO/UK military base outside the immediate war zone (Israel/Lebanon/Gulf). The attack significantly expands the conflict's geographic reach into Europe's doorstep. No casualties confirmed. Cyprus has previously activated its EU mutual defense clause, and UK bases have been used for repatriation flights.

description: Iran's largest sports complex, the 12,000-seat indoor arena at Tehran's Azadi Indoor Stadium, was destroyed by an American-Israeli strike. Israel launched another wave of attacks on Tehran.

target_text: Ceyhan oil pipeline
description: In Azerbaijan, an alleged Iranian-linked terrorist plot against the Baku–Tbilisi–Ceyhan oil pipeline, the Israeli embassy and synagogues in Baku is prevented by local law enforcement, with the trial of said alleged terrorists starting on the same day, during which they were identified as being part of a cell of the Islamic State known as "Vilayati-Khorasan" and the IRGC.

target_text: Hezbollah Executive Council Command Center + UAV Storage Facility
description: IDF conducted a broad-scale wave of 26 strikes in the Dahiyeh area targeting Hezbollah infrastructure. Targets included a Hezbollah executive council command center and a UAV storage facility used for attacks against Israel. IDF had earlier issued forced evacuation orders for all 500,000 residents of Beirut southern suburbs. Lebanese health ministry: 123 killed, 683 wounded in Lebanon since Monday.
source_urls: IDF official statement | Guardian Day 7 liveblog (01:29 AM EST March 6, 2026)

target_text: Hotel and residential buildings — central Manama
description: Iran targeted a hotel and two residential buildings in Manama, Bahrain early Friday March 6 (Day 7). Bahrain Interior Ministry: "No loss of life were recorded. Civil defence extinguished a fire in a flat in 1 of the 2 buildings." No casualties confirmed.
source_urls: Bahrain Interior Ministry (official X post) | DW liveblog March 6 2026

target_text: IRIS Shahid Bagheri — Iranian Drone Carrier
description: US CENTCOM struck and set ablaze the IRIS Shahid Bagheri — Iran's dedicated drone carrier ship, a converted container ship with a 180-meter runway for launching drones. Adm. Brad Cooper described it as "roughly the size of a World War II aircraft carrier." CENTCOM released black-and-white footage of the burning carrier. The Shahid Bagheri was inaugurated in 2025 with a range of 22,000 nautical miles. Its destruction eliminates Iran's primary sea-based drone launch platform. Earlier in the week the US submarine sank IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka (87 killed). Iran did not immediately acknowledge the attack. Per Hegseth Day 7 CENTCOM briefing: US has now sunk 30+ Iranian ships including this drone carrier.
source_urls: US CENTCOM official statement + black-and-white footage released (March 6, 2026 early Friday): confirmed strike on IRIS Shahid Bagheri, vessel on fire | AP News (March 6, 2026): "The U.S. military said early Friday that it struck an Iranian drone carrier, setting it ablaze. The U.S. military's Central Command released black-and-white footage of the burning carrier." Adm. Brad Cooper: "roughly the size of a World War II aircraft carrier. And as we speak, it's on fire."

target_text: Iran said it targeted Kurdish groups in Iraq and warned separatists amid the exp
description: Iran said it targeted Kurdish groups in Iraq and warned separatists amid the expanding conflict. Early on 5 March, Iran launched another round of missiles at Israel, setting off air-raid alerts in areas including Tel Aviv, though no casualties were immediately reported.

target_text: Iranian Regime Military Infrastructure — Isfahan (15th Wave)
description: IDF 15th wave of strikes on Iran: Israeli Air Force targets Iranian regime military infrastructure in both Tehran and Isfahan simultaneously. First confirmed strike on Isfahan in the current conflict — marking a geographic expansion of the campaign beyond Tehran. Isfahan hosts the Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center (ENTC) and major IRGC/defense production facilities. Further details expected. Day 7.
source_urls: IDF official statement | Times of Israel Day 7 liveblog

target_text: Sidon Building — Southern Lebanon (IDF airstrike)
description: Israeli airstrike hits a building on a main thoroughfare in Sidon (southern Lebanese coastal city, 40km south of Beirut) with no prior warning. Lebanon Health Ministry: at least 5 killed and 7 wounded. IDF has not commented. Strike expands Israeli Lebanon air campaign beyond Dahiyeh (Beirut southern suburbs) and border zone into Sidon — the third largest Lebanese city. Day 7.
source_urls: Lebanon Health Ministry | AFP via Times of Israel Day 7

target_text: Southern Israel — Beersheba Area (Iranian Ballistic Missile)
description: Iran fires a small salvo of ballistic missiles at southern Israel — sirens sound in Beersheba and surrounding area. Second Iranian missile launch of Day 7. IDF confirms detection; no reports of injuries or direct impacts. IDF Home Front Command: civilians may leave shelters but remain close. Day 7.
source_urls: IDF official statement | Times of Israel Day 7 liveblog

target_text: Tehran
description: In a leaked message, former Iranian foreign minister Mohammad Javad Zarif supported the strikes on the UAE, saying that the United Arab Emirates and Israel are 'one and the same'. Tehran accused the UAE of being the launch point of the US attack on Kharg Island on 13 March, urging an evacuation of three ports in the UAE in preparation for its response.

target_text: Tel Aviv — IRGC Wave of Drones and Missiles (Day 7 Morning)
description: IRGC launched a wave of drones and missiles targeting Tel Aviv on Day 7 Friday morning. The IDF confirmed it detected missile launches from Iran and air-raid sirens sounded across Tel Aviv. No immediate reports of major damage — the IDF confirmed launches but early reports indicated no significant impacts. This is the second confirmed Day 7 Iranian attack on Israel (after the Beersheba-area ballistic missile salvo logged as s155) and follows the overnight Day 6/7 transition Tel Aviv strike (s142). Iran continues to demonstrate a residual launch capability despite CENTCOM reporting 90% missile degradation and 83% drone degradation from Day 1. The IRGC formally announced the attack via IRNA. Day 7.
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 6, 2026): "The Israeli military said that it had detected missile launches from Iran" — NYT Day 7 liveblog pinned summary (March 6, 2026, 9:47 AM EST): "Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps also launched a wave of drones and missiles at targets in Tel Aviv on Friday, according to a statement from the force reported by IRNA, the country's state news agency. Air-raid sirens went off in the city, and the Israeli military said that it had detected missile launches from Iran, though there were no immediate reports of major damage." | IRNA (Iran state news agency, March 6, 2026): IRGC statement announcing "a combined missile and drone attack targeting locations in Tel Aviv" — confirmed by NYT Day 7 liveblog (March 6, 2026); IDF confirmed detection of missile launches from Iran. 2 sources: IDF official + IRNA/IRGC via NYT.

target_text: The Iranian ambassador to the United Nations wrote letters to the Security Counc
description: The Iranian ambassador to the United Nations wrote letters to the Security Council president and the UN secretary-general demanding reparations from Qatar for allowing its territory to be used for attacks on Iran.

target_text: The foreign ministers of G7 condemned Iran’s "unjustifiable" and "reckless" atta
description: The foreign ministers of G7 condemned Iran’s "unjustifiable" and "reckless" attacks on sites in the region.

target_text: Three Ghanaian peacekeepers enrolled in UNIFIL were hit by a missile and wounded
description: Three Ghanaian peacekeepers enrolled in UNIFIL were hit by a missile and wounded in Southern Lebanon. Responsibility for the attack was unclear.

target_text: Underground Ballistic Missile Bunkers — IRGC Missile Command
description: US B-2 Spirit stealth bombers dropped dozens of 2,000-pound GBU-57A/B Massive Ordnance Penetrator (MOP) or GBU-28 bunker-buster bombs on deeply buried ballistic missile storage and launcher bunkers inside Iran under cover of darkness on Day 7. CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirmed the mission — the first confirmed Phase 2 bunker-busting operation. Witnesses in and around Kermanshah reported intense explosions shaking their homes, in an area known to host multiple IRGC ballistic missile bases and storage facilities. This confirms Israel's Phase 2 transition (announced by IDF Chief Zamir March 6) moving from surface target destruction to hardened underground infrastructure. The B-2 is the only US aircraft capable of carrying MOP bombs against deeply buried targets. Cooper simultaneously announced US struck Iran's Space Command equivalent.
source_urls: US CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper (Day 7 press briefing, March 6, 2026): "Under cover of darkness Friday morning, B-2 stealth bombers dropped dozens of 2,000 pound penetrator bombs on deeply buried ballistic missile launchers inside Iran" | AP News (March 6, 2026): confirmed B-2 stealth bomber penetrator bomb campaign on deeply buried underground missile bunkers; witnesses reported explosions in the Kermanshah area, which hosts multiple Iranian missile bases

target_text: at Prince Sultan Air Base
description: Iran opened a new and wider campaign on Gulf states with attacks on Saudi Arabia (3 missiles had been intercepted at Prince Sultan Air Base), Kuwait (with drones and missiles), Qatar (where 13 missiles were intercepted), Bahrain (where a hotel and two residential buildings were targeted), and the UAE.

target_text: petroleum refinery
description: An explosion near Manama sparked a fire near a petroleum refinery and resulted in injuries to at least 32 Bahraini citizens, four of them "serious cases". An analysis by academic researchers examined by Reuters found that an American-operated Patriot missile was likely involved in the blast after it downed an Iranian drone mid-air. The UAE said that it intercepted 12 ballistic missiles and 17 drones from Iran.

target_text: Abu Ali Riyan — Hezbollah Radwan Force South Lebanon Sector Commander
description: IDF airstrike killed Abu Ali Riyan — commander of the south Lebanon sector in Hezbollah's elite Radwan Force. Managed Radwan Force operations, recruiting, and weapons supply chain. The Radwan Force had previously been tasked with invading Israel via the Conquer the Galilee plan. Strike conducted Saturday March 7; announced March 12.
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 12, 2026) | Times of Israel Day 13 liveblog (March 12, 2026)

target_text: Al Dhafra Air Base — US/UAE Military Base (IRGC Drone Swarm)
description: Day 8: IRGC Navy drone unit launched drone swarm attack on Al Dhafra Air Base south of Abu Dhabi — the largest US air base in the UAE, hosting F-35As and B-52s. UAE MoD confirmed: 121 UAVs detected, 119 intercepted, 2 fell within UAE territory. Attack came shortly after President Pezeshkian apologized to Gulf neighbors and vowed to stop attacking them — immediately undermining the diplomatic gesture.
source_urls: Tasnim News Agency (Iran official) | UAE Ministry of Defence (official X post) | CNBC

target_text: Dubai International Airport
description: Dubai International Airport briefly closed after an unidentified object was intercepted in the area, causing smoke to rise from the airport. Smoke was seen rising from the 23 Marina tower in the Dubai Marina after being hit by falling debris from an intercepted object. A Pakistani national was confirmed to have died after falling debris struck a vehicle in the Al Barsha area of the city.

target_text: Following news of the attack, international oil prices increased from $103 a bar
description: Following news of the attack, international oil prices increased from $103 a barrel to $108. Gas prices in Europe increased by 7 percent amid fears of Iranian retaliatory action against energy infrastructure in the Gulf Arab states. Iran halted its gas flows to Iraq, which relies on Iran for between a third and 40 percent of its gas and power needs.

target_text: Haifa Oil Refinery — Israeli Energy Infrastructure (IRGC Ballistic Missile)
description: Day 8: IRGC launched ballistic missile attack on Haifa, targeting Israel's Haifa oil refinery in declared retaliation for the US-Israeli strike on the Tehran oil depot (s158). IRGC: "The Guards said they had targeted Israel's Haifa oil refinery." Also includes broader ballistic missile wave on Haifa area per IRGC statement.
source_urls: IRGC official statement | The Hindu | NYT Day 8

target_text: President Masoud Pezeshkian issued an apology on state television for waves of I
description: President Masoud Pezeshkian issued an apology on state television for waves of Iranian attacks against neighbouring countries, stating that Iran had not attacked "friendly and neighboring countries".

target_text: Ras Laffan
description: QatarEnergy reported that same-day Iranian retaliatory missile strikes on Ras Laffan Industrial City caused "extensive damage." Qatar's ministry of interior said that it had brought a fire under control and that no injuries were reported. The Qatari foreign ministry strongly condemned "the brutal" Iranian attack on Ras Laffan Industrial City, considering it "a dangerous escalation, a flagrant violation of its sovereignty, and a direct threat to its national security." Qatar also declared the Iranian embassy's military and security personnel and staff as persona non grata. United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned "in the strongest terms" the Iranian strike on Qatari gas facilities.

target_text: Saudi Arabia said its air defenses had intercepted four ballistic missiles launc
description: Saudi Arabia said its air defenses had intercepted four ballistic missiles launched toward Riyadh and another two launched toward the Eastern Province. It reported four injuries as a result of falling shrapnel from an intercepted ballistic missile. Saudi foreign minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud said his country's patience with "Iranian aggression is not unlimited" and that trust in Iran has been "completely shattered."

target_text: South Pars
description: Israel conducted airstrikes on the South Pars natural gas field as well as oil and petrochemical facilities in Asaluyeh, according to Iranian state media, blaming both Israel and the United States. Although it initially objected Israeli targeting of Iranian oil sites, the United States approved the attack and coordinated with Israel, supporting the strike as a message to Iran over its closure of the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. president Donald Trump has ruled out any further strikes on Iranian energy sites, however.

target_text: Tehran
description: Israel first targeted several Iranian fuel sites in Tehran and the neighboring Karaj on 7 March 2026. Israel confirmed its "significant strike" targeting fuel storage and energy complexes in the capital, claiming they were being used by the Iranian Armed Forces. The United States opposed the Israeli strikes on oil facilities, expressing concern that the strikes could backfire strategically by producing support for the Islamic Republic. During the bombing raid on Kharg Island on 13 March, the United States struck military targets but spared the oil infrastructure "for reasons of decency."

target_text: The IRGC said that it attacked the Malta-flagged oil tanker Prima with a drone a
description: The IRGC said that it attacked the Malta-flagged oil tanker Prima with a drone after "ignoring repeated warnings from the IRGC Navy regarding the prohibition of traffic and the unsafe nature of the Strait of Hormuz".

target_text: The US approved a $151M arms sale to Israel, and sent a third aircraft carrier,
description: The US approved a $151M arms sale to Israel, and sent a third aircraft carrier, , to the Middle East.

target_text: The compounds and oil refineries structures of the US-based firm Halliburton loc
description: The compounds and oil refineries structures of the US-based firm Halliburton located in Basra are hit by Iranian strikes.

target_text: These targets include SAMREF refinery
description: The IRGC issued evacuation orders for several oil facilities across Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, and Qatar, deeming them "direct and legitimate targets and will be targeted in the coming hours." These targets include SAMREF refinery and Jubail petrochemical complex in Saudi Arabia, Al Hosn gas field in the UAE, and Ras Laffan refinery and Mesaieed petrochemical complex in Qatar.

target_text: U.S. Embassy Baghdad — Katyusha Rocket Attack
description: Day 8: Katyusha rockets targeted the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad; sirens sounded. Air defense systems intercepted rockets: 4 rockets launched toward the embassy per Iraqi security official; air defenses intercepted 3, one fell in an open area in the embassy airbase. No casualties reported. Attack attributed to Iran-backed Iraqi militia groups.
source_urls: AFP | Reuters | The Hindu | Iraqi security sources

target_text: UAE Targets — IRGC Day 8 Missile and Drone Wave (Dubai + others)
description: Day 8: Iran continued firing missiles and drones at UAE on Saturday despite President Pezeshkian earlier apologizing and vowing to halt attacks on Gulf neighbors. Loud explosions heard in Dubai. UAE MoD confirmed: 16 ballistic missiles detected (15 intercepted, 1 fell into sea) + 121 UAVs (119 intercepted, 2 fell within UAE territory). Cumulative since Feb 28: 221 ballistic missiles and ~1,200 drones fired at UAE. Interception debris from one aerial intercept killed an Asian driver — the 4th UAE death total. UAE wounded rose to 112. UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed gave rare televised address calling UAE "in a period of war" and warning enemies of an "Iron Fist."
source_urls: UAE Ministry of Defense (official X post, March 7 2026) | Fox News/AP (March 7 2026) | The Hindu/AFP (March 7-8 2026)

target_text: drones struck Iraqi airport
description: Late on Friday, drones struck Iraqi airports and oil facilities; in Basra, a compound housing foreign oil-company workers, including Halliburton and KBR facilities, was hit and caught fire.

target_text: targeted supply and transmission pipeline
description: According to Fars News Agency, Israel struck petrochemical facilities in Asaluyeh, damaging storage tanks, gas installations, and refineries. The attack targeted supply and transmission pipelines and took place around 2:10 p.m. local time. Iran's Ministry of Petroleum said that a number of facilities were damaged and that a fire at the gas field was being put under control. No casualties were immediately reported. The attack halted output at two refineries with a combined capacity of 100 million cubic meters per day. A number of phases at the gas field were shut down to stop the fire from spreading.

target_text: targeting its Habshan gas facility
description: The UAE said its air defenses dealt with 13 ballistic missiles and 27 drones from Iran. The UAE condemned the "Iranian terrorist attack" targeting its Habshan gas facility, stating that it constitutes a "dangerous escalation and a violation of the principles of international law."

target_text: An Iranian drone strike caused a fire in a Kuwait City tower belonging to the Pu
description: An Iranian drone strike caused a fire in a Kuwait City tower belonging to the Public Institution for Social Security. Kuwait reported that two of its officers were killed while performing duties.

target_text: Diplomatic Quarter + Riyadh City — Drone Wave (Intercepted)
description: Saudi air defenses intercepted multiple drone waves targeting Riyadh early Day 9 (Sunday). Confirmed: 1 drone targeting Diplomatic Quarter (thwarted, no damage); 6 drones targeting Riyadh city (intercepted); 4 drones east of Riyadh (intercepted); 1 drone heading to Shaybah oil field in Empty Quarter (intercepted). Total: 12 drones. No material damage or casualties. Second attempted attack on the Diplomatic Quarter after the US Embassy strike (Day 3).
source_urls: Saudi Ministry of Defense (Maj. Gen. Al-Maliki official statement, March 8 2026) | Saudi Gazette (March 8 2026)

target_text: IRGC Air Force Headquarters — Command Center Managing All Iranian Missile & Drone Fire
description: The Israeli Air Force destroyed the headquarters of Iran's IRGC Air Force in Tehran. IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin confirmed the destruction in a press conference on Day 9 (March 8, 2026). Defrin stated that Iran 'managed the fire of missiles and drones at Israel and countries in the region' from this headquarters. According to IDF Farsi social media, the headquarters included: command and control units for the IRGC's ballistic missile units, drone units, and other air force components. The destruction of the IRGC Air Force HQ eliminates the primary command-and-control infrastructure Iran was using to coordinate its ongoing missile and drone barrages against Israel, Gulf states, and US bases. This strike follows the destruction of the IRGC Space Force headquarters (Khayyam satellite command, s185) and is part of the Day 9 afternoon 'extensive strikes' wave (s184/s186). Combined with the 90% missile degradation and 83% drone degradation confirmed by CENTCOM, the loss of the IRGC Air Force command center represents a significant degradation of Iran's remaining offensive coordination capability. Specific location within Tehran not disclosed; Defrin said it was in Tehran. Day 9, March 8, 2026.
source_urls: IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin (press conference, March 8, 2026): 'The Israeli Air Force destroyed the headquarters of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' air force in Tehran during a recent wave of strikes.' Defrin: Iran 'managed the fire of missiles and drones at Israel and countries in the region' from the headquarters. — Times of Israel Day 9 liveblog (March 8, 2026) | Times of Israel Day 9 liveblog (March 8, 2026): 'Israel destroys Tehran HQ of IRGC's air force, which managed missile fire — IDF.' IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin press conference. 2 sources: IDF official + ToI.

target_text: IRGC Space Force Headquarters — Khayyam Satellite Command & Control Center
description: IDF struck the IRGC Space Force headquarters in Tehran — the command-and-control center for the Khayyam surveillance satellite launched August 2022. IDF: "The site included research facilities as well as a command-and-control structure for the Khayyam satellite, which was used by the IRGC to advance terrorist activities and monitor the State of Israel and its residents." The headquarters served as a reception, transmission, and research center for the Iranian Space Agency, which is affiliated with the Iranian military.
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 8, 2026) | CBS News (March 8, 2026) | AP (March 8, 2026)

target_text: Iran — IDF Day 9 Afternoon "Extensive Strikes": Regime Infrastructure Tehran + Nationwide
description: IDF announced it has begun "extensive strikes" against the "infrastructure" of the Iranian regime in Tehran and across other areas in Iran on Sunday afternoon (Day 9). The announcement was made around 12:00 PM EDT (17:00 UTC) on March 8. This represents a new afternoon wave of strikes following: the overnight oil facility attacks (s180, 5 facilities, 4 killed), the Qeshm desalination plant strike (s183), and multiple prior Day 9 morning waves. The IDF use of "extensive" mirrors the language of previous high-intensity waves. Specific targets not immediately disclosed. Day 9 afternoon.
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 8, 2026, ~12:00 PM EDT / 17:00 UTC): IDF has begun "extensive strikes" against the "infrastructure" of the Iranian regime in Tehran and across other areas in Iran — The Guardian Day 9 liveblog (March 8, 2026, 12.00 EDT entry) | The Guardian Day 9 liveblog (March 8, 2026): "The IDF has said in the past hour that it begun 'extensive strikes' against the 'infrastructure' of the Iranian regime in Tehran and across other areas in Iran" — confirms IDF official statement

target_text: Iranian Military Fuel Depots (Multiple Locations)
description: Day 9 early hours (early Sunday UTC): IDF struck several Iranian fuel depots around Tehran and in the neighboring city of Karaj (west of Tehran), sending huge columns of fire and smoke climbing into the air and rocking both cities with explosions. NYT reported an enormous fire at an oil storage facility northeast of Tehran and explosions from an oil depot south of the city — confirming multiple separate depot locations struck simultaneously. The IDF specifically stated the fuel depots were targeted because they were being used by Irans military forces to operate military infrastructure. This is the first wave of Day 9 (March 8) and continues the Phase 2 strategy of targeting Irans logistics and military supply chain infrastructure beyond leadership targets.
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 8 2026 — targeting fuel depots used by Irans military) | New York Times Day 9 liveblog (March 8 2026 — multiple fuel depots struck around Tehran + Karaj) | AP (March 8 2026)

target_text: Iranian Regime Infrastructure — Day 9 New Wave
description: IDF launches new wave of strikes across Iran in early Sunday hours, targeting Iranian regime infrastructure. Iran responded with 4 separate missile salvos fired at Israel since midnight March 8 — rocket trails seen above Netanya per CBS News aerial footage. IDF intercepted incoming and launched counter-strikes. Day 9.
source_urls: IDF official statement | Times of Israel Day 9 liveblog (March 8, 2026) | CBS News (March 8, 2026)

target_text: Isfahan Airport — Iranian F-14 Tomcat Fighters Destroyed
description: IDF Air Force carried out a broad wave of airstrikes on Isfahan Airport, destroying several Iranian Grumman F-14 Tomcat fighters stored there. The F-14s were originally supplied to Iran by the US before the 1979 Islamic Revolution. IDF also destroyed air defense detection equipment at the site. Combined with the Day 9 strike destroying 16 IRGC Quds Force aircraft at Mehrabad, the IDF says these operations are designed to strengthen Israel's aerial superiority over Iran. Part of Phase 2 expansion to Isfahan (along with the Day 7 ENTC nuclear technology center strikes). Day 9.
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 8, 2026) | Times of Israel Day 9 liveblog (March 8, 2026)

target_text: Mehrabad International Airport — 16 IRGC Aircraft Destroyed
description: Day 9 (early Sunday): IDF struck Mehrabad International Airport in Tehran overnight. IDF official statement (March 8, 2026) confirmed 16 aircraft used by the IRGC Quds Force were destroyed at the site. Planet Labs PBC satellite imagery published by NBC News separately confirmed more than a dozen aircraft destroyed, pieces of planes scattered across scorched tarmac. Mehrabad is one of two main airports in the Iranian capital (the other is Imam Khomeini International). Al Jazeera correspondent Tohid Asadi reported from Tehran that the nearby area was also affected. The IDF said the strikes are designed to further Israel's aerial superiority over Iran. Sources: IDF official statement (March 8, 2026); NBC News + Planet Labs PBC satellite imagery.
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 8, 2026) | NBC News | Planet Labs PBC satellite imagery

target_text: Multiple Regime Infrastructure Targets — IDF Afternoon Extensive Strike Wave
description: IDF launched an "extensive strikes" wave against Iranian regime infrastructure in Tehran and across other areas in Iran on Day 9 afternoon (March 8, 2026). IDF statement: "The IDF has begun extensive strikes against the infrastructure of the Iranian regime in Tehran and across other areas in Iran." This follows the Parchin/Shahroud Phase 2 targeting confirmed by Brig. Gen. Defrin and the overnight oil depot strikes. Netanyahu vowed to strike Iran's rulers "without mercy" with an "organised plan with many surprises." Trump told ABC News the US is "ahead of schedule both in terms of lethality and in terms of time."
source_urls: IDF Official Statement | Guardian Day 9 liveblog

target_text: Netanya Area — Iranian Missile Volley (Day 9 Response to IDF Wave)
description: Iran fired a volley of missiles toward Israel in early Day 9 Sunday (approximately 09:30-10:00 UTC), prompting Israeli air defense systems to activate. Rocket trails were photographed above Netanya, Israel (AFP photographer Jack Guez). IDF sent emergency alerts to impacted regions across the country. The Iranian volley appears to be a direct response to the IDF launching a new broad wave of strikes across Iran in the same time window (s178). IDF confirmed air defense systems were operating to intercept the threat. Day 9.
source_urls: CBS News liveblog (March 8, 2026 12:12 AM ET): missile volley from Iran toward Israel, Israeli air defense systems activated; rocket trails visible above Netanya | IDF official statement (March 8, 2026): Iran fired volley of missiles toward Israel, emergency alerts sent to impacted regions; intercepted

target_text: New IDF Wave — Iranian Regime Infrastructure (Day 9 Early Sunday)
description: IDF launched a new broad wave of strikes across Iran targeting Iranian regime military infrastructure in western and central Iran on early Day 9 Sunday morning (approximately 9:30 UTC / 5:30 AM ET). IDF officially confirmed this wave targeted over 400 Iranian regime military infrastructure sites, using numerous munitions including against ballistic missile launchers and additional weapons production sites. IDF summary stat: since Operation Roaring Lion began, IAF has carried out approximately 190 strikes targeting Iran's ballistic missile launchers, air defense systems, and weapons storage facilities. Shortly after the IDF announcement, Iran launched missiles toward Israel in response (s179), with Israeli air defense systems activating to intercept the threat. IDF also confirmed in this operational period: destruction of F-14 Tomcat fighters stored at Isfahan airport (s177) and 16 IRGC Quds Force aircraft destroyed at Mehrabad (s167). Sources: IDF official statement + Fox News liveblog (March 8, 2026).
source_urls: IDF official statement (March 8, 2026) | Fox News liveblog (March 8, 2026) | IDF official statement (Fox News March 8, 2026 — 400+ sites confirmed, 190 IAF launcher/AD strikes total)

target_text: Parchin + Shahroud Ballistic and Cruise Missile Production Sites — Phase 2 Expansion
description: Day 9: IDF Military Spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin confirmed that Israel's Phase 2 campaign has expanded targeting to Parchin and Shahroud — two of Iran's most critical ballistic and cruise missile production and research facilities. Defrin: "The operation has now moved into a new phase focused on expanding strikes on major regime production sites, including facilities in Parchin and Shahroud tied to ballistic and cruise missile production." Parchin Military Complex (35km SE of Tehran) houses Iran's main conventional warhead manufacturing, solid-fuel propellant production, and warhead design programs — long suspected of hosting covert nuclear weaponization research (IAEA has sought access for over a decade). Shahroud Missile Research and Development Center (northeast Iran) is Iran's primary space launch and long-range ballistic missile test complex, used to test the Shahab, Sajjil, and Khorramshahr missile families. Phase 2 targeting of Parchin and Shahroud represents a major escalation beyond the Phase 1 focus on leadership decapitation and air defense destruction — directly targeting Iran's missile manufacturing supply chain and R&D infrastructure. Sources: IDF Brig. Gen. Defrin (NBC News, March 8, 2026); 2 sources confirmed.
source_urls: IDF Military Spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin | NBC News

target_text: Qeshm Island Desalination Plant — Water Infrastructure
description: US forces struck a desalination plant on Qeshm Island, cutting water supply for approximately 30 villages, according to Iranian FM Araghchi. Araghchi called it "a dangerous move with grave consequences" and said the US "set this precedent, not Iran," in response to Iran's own desalination plant strike in Bahrain. Qeshm Island is in the Strait of Hormuz, adjacent to Bandar Abbas. This is the first confirmed US/Israel strike on Iranian water infrastructure in the conflict. Iran has pre-existing severe water shortages making this particularly impactful. Two sources: Araghchi official X post (ISNA relayed) + Guardian (March 8, 2026).
source_urls: Araghchi X post | The Guardian | NYT Day 9

target_text: Residential Building — Sidon District
description: Israeli airstrike on Tefahta village in the Sidon district, south Lebanon. Lebanon Health Ministry confirmed 5 killed; rescue teams searching the site with the ministry noting the fatality count is not final.

target_text: Residential Compound — Al-Kharj Governorate (First Lethal Strike on Saudi Civilians)
description: Iranian military projectile strikes residential compound in Al-Kharj governorate, Saudi Arabia — first lethal attack on Saudi civilian soil since conflict began. 2 killed (1 Indian national + 1 Bangladeshi national), 12 wounded (all Bangladeshi nationals). Qatar FM called it a dangerous escalation. First confirmed civilian fatalities from Iranian attacks on Saudi territory.
source_urls: Saudi Arabia Civil Defense (official statement, March 8, 2026): confirmed a military projectile landed in a residential compound in Al-Kharj governorate — 2 killed (1 Indian national + 1 Bangladeshi national), 12 wounded (all Bangladeshi nationals). First confirmed civilian deaths from Iranian attacks on Saudi soil. | NBC News liveblog (March 8, 2026, 12:48 PM EDT): Saudi Civil Defense official confirms 2 killed, 12 wounded in Al-Kharj residential compound. Qatar FM condemned the attack calling it a dangerous escalation. 2 sources confirmed.

target_text: Seafront Hotel — Central Beirut: 4 Killed, 10 Wounded
description: Day 9: Israeli airstrike struck a hotel on the seafront (Corniche area) in central Beirut, killing at least 4 people and wounding 10 others per Lebanon Health Ministry. An AFP photographer at the seafront hotel saw shattered windows in one of the rooms, while security forces sealed off the surrounding area. The IDF announced it had "begun an additional wave of strikes in Beirut" targeting Hezbollah positions in the Lebanese capital, with southern suburbs (Dahiyeh, Hezbollah stronghold) listed as the primary focus. The central Beirut hotel strike represents continued expansion of IDF targeting in Lebanon. This is the second hotel struck in the conflict — after the Hazmieh hotel strike (s111, Day 5). IDF official statement (Fox News + Guardian Day 9, March 8 2026): IDF confirmed this was a "precision strike" targeting "key commanders" of the Quds Force Lebanon Corps operating out of the hotel. The commanders were "working to advance terror plots against Israel from Lebanese territory." Steps taken to minimize civilian casualties per IDF. Lebanon Health Ministry: 4 killed, 10 injured. Lebanon PM Salam called strike first to "hit the heart of the Lebanese capital since hostilities resumed." IDF confirmed Day 9 (March 8, 2026) that the target was key commanders of Iran's Revolutionary Guards Quds Force sheltering in the hotel — marking a significant geographic expansion of Israeli leadership targeting in Beirut beyond the Dahiyeh and southern suburbs. IDF: "targeted key commanders of Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps [Quds Force] in Beirut." The Rawche area is a civilian seafront district near Pigeons Rock — distinct from the Dahiyeh Hezbollah stronghold. UPDATED March 8, 2026 (IDF press conference, Brig. Gen. Zamir + Defrin): IDF named 5 IRGC Quds Force commanders killed in this strike, all 'while hiding in a civilian hotel': (1) Majid Hassini — senior moneyman in IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps, responsible for transferring funds from Iran to Hezbollah and Hamas; (2) Ali Reza Bi-Azar — chief of intelligence, IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps; (3) Ahmad Rasouli — chief of intelligence, IRGC Quds Force Palestine Corps; (4) Hossein Ahmadlou — lower-ranking intelligence operative, IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps; (5) Abu Muhammad Ali — Hezbollah's representative in the Palestine Corps. All five were confirmed killed. The initial Lebanon Health Ministry report cited 4 killed; the IDF's full accounting confirms 5 IRGC officers. This strike also follows the earlier IRGC kills: Daoud Alizadeh (acting Lebanon Corps commander, struck Tehran); Reza Khazaei (chief of staff Lebanon Corps, prior Beirut strike). Sources: Times of Israel Day 9 liveblog (March 8, 2026); IDF official press conference Zamir/Defrin (March 8, 2026).

target_text: IDF Second Commando Operation — Nabi Sheet Village (Helicopter Raid from Syrian Border)
description: The IDF conducted a second helicopter commando operation at Nabi Sheet village in the Bekaa Valley, eastern Lebanon, in the early hours of Monday March 9 (Day 10). Hezbollah detected approximately 15 Israeli helicopters crossing from the Syrian border into eastern Lebanon. Hezbollah forces engaged the helicopters and landed troops. Two senior Hezbollah officials in the Bekaa confirmed to AFP that an Israeli helicopter was downed in the mountains east of Baalbek — a first confirmed Israeli aircraft loss in the Lebanon theater. The IDF did not immediately comment. This is the second commando operation at Nabi Sheet in as many days: the first (overnight Friday March 7-8) was aimed at recovering the remains of Ron Arad, an Israeli airman missing since 1986, but failed to find them and left 3 Lebanese soldiers and 41 Bekaa Valley residents dead per Lebanese army and health ministry. The second operation's objective is unknown. The use of the Syrian border crossing corridor suggests ongoing IDF coordination of Lebanon ground operations via Syrian territory.

Al Udeid is the largest US air base in the Middle East (~11,000 US personnel). Al Harir is a KRG/US military base in Erbil Governorate. No casualties reported in initial accounts.

target_text: Bahrain — New Iranian Attack Wave, Air Defense Sirens
description: Air defense sirens warned of incoming Iranian missiles in Bahrain on Day 11 Tuesday (March 10, 2026). No new casualties confirmed in initial reports. Bahrain has been under sustained Iranian attack since Day 1 — confirmed 3 killed and dozens wounded across multiple strike events including Sitra island drone strike (s189), BAPCO refinery (Day 10), and Manama residential building (s212). Day 11 (March 10, 2026).

target_text: Construction Site — Residential/Urban Area (Cluster Warhead)
description: Iranian ballistic missile carrying a cluster warhead struck a construction site and surrounding areas in Yehud (also known as Yehud-Monosson), a city in central Israel east of Tel Aviv near Ben Gurion International Airport. Two Israeli civilians were killed in the blast — the second cluster munitions attack on civilian areas in Israel in two days (the first being the Day 10 cluster bomb on central Israel, 1 killed). First responders responded to the construction site impact. The attack is part of Iran's Day 11 overnight missile barrage on central Israel. Yehud is the seat of the Dan Regional Council and has a population of approximately 40,000. The use of cluster munitions by Iran against civilian areas of Israel follows their use in Day 10 and raises additional IHL concerns. IDF confirmed missiles launched from Iran and interceptors deployed. Day 11 (March 10, 2026).
source_urls: AJC (American Jewish Committee, published March 10, 2026): "On March 10, an Iranian missile carrying a cluster warhead struck multiple sites across central Israel, killing two civilians in the city of Yehud as part of several missile barrages launched overnight targeting civilian areas south and east." | Times of Israel (Day 11 liveblog, March 10, 2026): Photo caption — "First responders at the scene of a deadly missile impact at a construction site in Yehud on March 9-10, 2026." (March 9 in Israeli local time = March 10 UTC). IDF confirmed missiles detected from Iran targeting Israel. 3 sources: AJC + ToI + IDF statement.

target_text: Dubai International Airport
description: Dubai International Airport closed briefly while passengers and staff were directed to bomb shelters after defense systems alerted of incoming missile and drone attacks. Australia said it would send a Boeing E-7 Wedgetail EWACS aircraft and missiles to the UAE but would not deploy troops. UAE air defenses reported that it intercepted multiple ballistic missiles and drones.

target_text: Fujairah
description: Two oil storage tanks that were severely damage in Fujairah were burning and releasing dark plumes of smoke into the sky, while another had burned.

target_text: Haifa Oil Refineries and Fuel Storage — Iranian Missile Strike After 9-Hour Break
description: Iran launched a new round of ballistic missiles toward Haifa, Israel on Day 11 morning (March 10, 2026) — notably the first such attack after a nine-hour break in launches. Iran's army specifically stated it targeted Haifa's oil refineries and fuel storage. Sky News analyst Alistair Bunkall: "The tempo of attacks from Iran has gone down — the question we can't yet answer is whether or not that is entirely because Iran's missile stocks and ability to launch missiles have been degraded, or whether Iran is keeping its powder dry to elongate the war." Israeli air defense systems were activated; interception status pending. Day 11 (March 10, 2026).
source_urls: Sky News Day 11 liveblog (March 10, 2026, ~09:57 UTC): "A new round of missiles has been launched from Iran towards Israel this morning, the Israeli military has announced. Iran's army said it had targeted refineries and fuel storage in the city of Haifa. It is, however, notably the first such attack after a nine-hour break." | IDF official statement (March 10, 2026): detected Iranian missile launch toward Israel, air defense systems activated; Sky News cites IDF as confirming the detection.

target_text: Kataeb Imam Ali (Pro-Iran Militia) — Airstrike, 4 Killed
description: Four fighters from the Iran-backed Kataeb Imam Ali militia were killed in airstrikes in northern Iraq on Tuesday Day 11 (March 10, 2026). The armed faction itself confirmed the deaths and attributed the strikes to the United States. Kataeb Imam Ali is a pro-Iran Shia militia within the Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) umbrella. This is a separate incident from the 40th Brigade PMF airstrike in Kirkuk (s218, 5 killed). Pro-Iran Iraqi militias have been launching attacks on US bases throughout the conflict. Day 11 (March 10, 2026).

target_text: Kuwait — 6 Iranian Drones Intercepted by National Guard
description: Kuwait's National Guard confirmed it shot down six Iranian drones in the early hours of Day 11 (March 10, 2026). Kuwait has been under sustained Iranian attack since Day 1. This is the largest single confirmed intercept count for Kuwait in the conflict. Kuwait hosts US military logistics and the Shuaiba port facility (site of the Day 1 US Army Reservist deaths). Kuwait emir confirmed 12 Kuwaiti nationals killed total as of Day 10. No new casualties from this wave. Day 11 (March 10, 2026).
source_urls: Kuwait National Guard (official, March 10, 2026): shot down six drones | AP (published March 10, 2026 ~06:00 UTC): "Kuwait's National Guard said it had shot down six drones"
