target_text: On 7 March, the Ministry of Interior announced the arrest of "a person of Arab n
description: On 7 March, the Ministry of Interior announced the arrest of "a person of Arab nationality after he posted a series of tweets on one of his social media accounts that contained direct insults toward the State of Qatar and its institutions". On the same day, the ministry warned the public to "comply with the laws and regulations and to refrain from using social media or digital platforms to spread rumors, incite unrest, defame the State of Qatar and its institutions", and called on the public to "report any such behavior". On 9 March, it announced that 313 foreigners had been arrested for having "filmed and circulated video clips and published misleading information and rumours that could stir public opinion".

target_text: Residential Building — Arak, Markazi Province
description: US-Israeli airstrike struck a residential building in Arak (capital of Markazi Province, western Iran), killing at least 5 people and injuring several others. ISNA reported confirmed civilian casualties. Arak hosts IRGC facilities and the Arak Heavy Water Nuclear Reactor (IR-40) complex, which has been under prior attack. This strike appears to have hit a residential area adjacent to or near military/nuclear infrastructure.
source_urls: ISNA (Iranian state news agency, March 10, 2026) | Al Jazeera Day 11 liveblog (March 10, 2026)

target_text: Residential Building — Central Manama
description: Iranian attack on a residential building in central Manama killed 1 person and injured others — confirmed by Bahrain Interior Ministry. Distinct from the earlier Sitra island drone attack (s189). Bahrain interior ministry: 'Initial reports indicate one person died and others were injured in a blatant Iranian attack targeting a residential building in the capital.'
source_urls: Bahrain Interior Ministry (official statement, March 10, 2026) | Times of Israel Day 10/11 liveblog (March 10, 2026)

target_text: Saudi Oil-Rich Eastern Region — 2 Iranian Drones Intercepted
description: Saudi Arabia Defense Ministry confirmed the destruction of two Iranian drones over the oil-rich Eastern Province on Day 11 morning. The Eastern Province contains the Aramco Ghawar oil field (world's largest), Ras Tanura export terminal, and ABQAIQ processing facility. No damage reported. Iran has been systematically targeting Saudi energy infrastructure throughout the conflict. Saudi Arabia has now intercepted hundreds of Iranian drones and missiles across the kingdom. Day 11 (March 10, 2026).
source_urls: Saudi Arabia Defense Ministry (official, March 10, 2026): destroyed two drones over the kingdom's oil-rich eastern region | AP (published March 10, 2026 ~06:00 UTC): "Saudi Arabia said it had destroyed two drones over its oil-rich eastern region"

target_text: Several reports were issued on the many pets being left abandoned in the streets
description: Several reports were issued on the many pets being left abandoned in the streets of Dubai by fleeing expats. Local veterinarians and pet hospitals described being overwhelmed by the abandoned pets. Some pets were left tied to poles or stranded in the desert. Others were reported being euthanized.

target_text: Syrian media reported that the Israelis intercepted Iranian drones in the towns

target_text: Tehran
description: HRANA reported 202 attacks across 19 provinces, with 53 casualties including at least 17 civilians killed and 19 injured, as well as 8 unclassified deaths. An attack on an intersection along Tehran's Resalat Highway resulted in a large number of civilian casualties. Other targets stuck included airbases in Bandar Abbas and Isfahan, an IRGC intelligence headquarters in Qom, the Kerman Airport fuel depot, a law enforcement building in Azarshahr, the Najafabad courthouse, a residential neighborhood in Mehrshahr, and a battery company and municipal informatics building in Tehran. Israel said that it struck a weapons research site in Tehran. The US said it struck multiple Iranian minelaying ships.

target_text: The Qatari Ministry of Foreign Affairs denounced the strikes, calling them a "re
description: The Qatari Ministry of Foreign Affairs denounced the strikes, calling them a "reckless and irresponsible" violation of Qatar's sovereignty. A spokesperson from the Foreign Affairs ministry stated that Qatar has the authority to respond "in accordance with international law".

target_text: The UNHCR reported that about 700 thousand people were displaced in Lebanon by I

target_text: UAE Consulate General — Iraqi Kurdistan
description: UAE consulate general in Iraqi Kurdistan targeted in a drone attack. UAE Foreign Ministry confirmed damage but no casualties. Statement: attack 'represents a dangerous escalation and a threat to regional security and stability'; 'targeting diplomatic missions and premises constitutes a flagrant violation of all international norms and laws.' Perpetrator not officially identified; suspected Iran-aligned Iraqi militia.
source_urls: UAE Foreign Ministry (official statement, March 10, 2026) | Times of Israel Day 10/11 liveblog (March 10, 2026)

description: 6 drones launched toward the US diplomatic support center (logistical hub for US diplomats near Baghdad International Airport) by Iran-backed Islamic Resistance in Iraq militia. One drone struck the facility, hitting near a guard tower. Five drones intercepted. No casualties — US State Dept issued internal alert ordering personnel to "duck and cover." Washington Post confirmed via US official + State Dept internal alert.

target_text: at the Ruwais Industrial Complex
description: A fire broke out at the Ruwais Industrial Complex in Abu Dhabi, which houses the country's largest oil refinery as a result of an Iranian drone strike. The fire caused Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) to shut off its refinery, a facility that would otherwise produce 922,000 barrels of oil per day.

target_text: were hit near the port
description: The bulk carrier Mayuree Naree (IMO 9323649) was hit by two missiles shortly after passing the Strait of Hormuz. The vessel caught fire and most of the crew abandoned ship. Marshall Islands-flagged bulk carrier Star Gwyneth (IMO 9301031) and Japan-flagged container vessel One Majesty (IMO 9424912) were also reported to have been damaged by unknown projectiles in the vicinity of the Strait. The spokesperson of the Iraqi armed forces, Lieutenant General Saad Maan, said that two foreign vessels were hit near the port of Al-Faw, one person was killed while 35 people were rescued.

target_text: Aisha Bakkar Residential Area — Central Beirut Apartment Strike

Lebanon's state-run National News Agency (NNA) reported the strike on Wednesday March 11 early morning. Aisha Bakkar is a predominantly residential district in west Beirut near Hamra. No immediate casualty count from initial NNA report. This marks an escalation of IDF strikes into Beirut's civilian center, beyond Dahiyeh (southern suburbs) where Hezbollah infrastructure is concentrated. target_text: Hezbollah infrastructure — new IDF widescale wave on central Beirut (Day 13 afternoon)
description: Israel launched a new widescale wave of strikes on central Beirut on Day 13 afternoon, targeting Hezbollah infrastructure. Strikes sent thick plumes of smoke above a residential area in the Ramlet al-Bayda / Hamra district — near high-end commercial areas, far from southern suburbs where Hezbollah traditionally operates. Lebanon PM Nawaf Salam condemned the strikes minutes later in a televised speech: "We cannot, under any circumstances, accept that Lebanon once again becomes an open arena for the wars of others." IDF also issued evacuation orders for a village ~64km east of Beirut. Casualties TBD.

target_text: IRGC/Basij security force checkpoints — multiple locations across Tehran
description: Drone attacks struck IRGC/security force checkpoints across Tehran for the first time in the conflict on Wednesday night. At least 10 people killed in suspected drone assaults. Israel and US did not immediately respond to comment. This marks the first targeting of domestic security checkpoint infrastructure in the conflict — IRGC and Basij units man these checkpoints as part of the crackdown on potential anti-regime protests.

target_text: Iraqi oil terminal

Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani confirmed the attack on X, saying he strongly condemned it but did not identify the perpetrator. All soldiers on the base were safe — no casualties. This follows the earlier UAE consulate in Erbil being struck by a drone (Day 11, s213-adjacent), extending the targeting of international presence in Iraqi Kurdistan.

target_text: On March 20 an analysis by Saad al-Kaabi, QatarEnergy's CEO and state minister f
description: On March 20 an analysis by Saad al-Kaabi, QatarEnergy's CEO and state minister for energy affairs, said that the Iranian attacks on the country's energy assets have disrupted about 17% of its liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, causing huge financial losses and damage that could take 3–5 years to repair.

target_text: PMF base
description: The Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) militia's 18th brigade headquarters in Al-Qa'im, Iraq, was hit by a direct strike. The overnight strikes on Al-Qa'im killed 15 people, while another strike on a PMF base near Kirkuk killed five people. The strikes on the PMF were condemned by the Iraqi government.

target_text: Ras Laffan
description: On 2 March, soon after the reported drone attack on Qatar's gas facilities in Ras Laffan and Mesaieed, QatarEnergy announced that it has ceased all production of natural gas and its associated products, reportedly on order by Energy Minister Saad Sherida al-Kaabi. These announcements caused sharp increases in world gas prices, which analysts said was a part of Iran's plan to apply pressure on the world to stop the war. On 6 March, al-Kaabi warned that if the war continues other Gulf energy producers may be forced to halt exports and declare Force Majeure, and that "this will bring down economies of the world". The events caused a jump in global oil and gas prices. Singapore and Bangladesh<!--add more--> sought replacements for the Qatari Force Majeure. LNG carrier rates went from $42,000 on 25 February, to over a quarter million dollars by 5 March, and then dropped rapidly as Qatari LNG carriers went unused in the Atlantic Ocean.

target_text: Tehran
description: 65% of the day's attacks hit Tehran, with the remainder in a geographically dispersed pattern. HRANA recorded 243 attacks and at least 66 casualties, including 21 civilians killed and 31 injured. Targets included the District 13 Air Force Base in Tehran, the Peykanshahr Basij Base, and Mehrabad International Airport, where the IDF said that it destroyed an aircraft used by senior Iranian officials, including former supreme leader Ali Khamenei. An electricity department building and eleven residential buildings in Tehran, including an eight-story building on Zarafshan Street, were destroyed in the strikes. Military targets in Chabahar and Shiraz, as well as the Shahed Imam Reza School in Khomein and a desalination plant in Nikshahr. Kata'ib Hezbollah announced the death of Abu Ali al-Askari, a senior commander who was reportedly killed in Baghdad.

target_text: The first two floors of the Royal Tulip Al-Rasheed Hotel in Baghdad's Green Zone
description: The first two floors of the Royal Tulip Al-Rasheed Hotel in Baghdad's Green Zone were hit by a drone whilst hosting a European Union and Saudi Arabian delegation. The attack was not claimed by anyone, however, due to the proximity of a similar attack that had happened 48 hours earlier against the US embassy in Baghdad, it is suspected to have been an Iranian attack.

target_text: attack at Dubai International Airport
description: A drone attack at Dubai International Airport which hit a fuel tank caused a fire, which resulted in temporary suspension of flights. It also led to the temporary close of roads near the airport. Some flights were redirected to Al Maktoum International Airport. The fire was extinguished later in the day. It was the third incident at the airport since Iran began striking the country on 28 February. Emirates Airlines resumed limited operations at 10:00 local time.

target_text: tank at Dubai International Airport
description: A drone attack set a fuel tank at Dubai International Airport on fire in the early morning. Flights were initially suspended, but service was restored over the course of the morning. A drone attack also caused a fire at the Port of Fujairah, and a Palestinian was killed by a rocket near Abu Dhabi.

description: As of 17 March 2026, Iran has fired 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, and 1,672 unmanned aerial vehicles at the UAE. Even though most of them were destroyed, interception debris and falling projectiles fell on populated areas in Abu Dhabi and Dubai causing damage to civilian infrastructure and starting fires.

target_text: As of 17 March 2026, eight people were killed, including two military personnel,
description: As of 17 March 2026, eight people were killed, including two military personnel, and 157 have been injured. The civilians killed were foreign nationals from Pakistan, Nepal, Bangladesh, and Palestine. On 7 March, a Pakistani driver was killed. The two military personnel killed were aboard a helicopter that crashed during a malfunction on 9 March.

The Fujairah Oil Industry Zone was hit by drones, resulting in a fire. A Pakistani national was killed in Baniyas from debris from an intercepted ballistic missile.

target_text: Tehran
description: HRANA recorded 179 attacks and at least 19 total casualties, included 3 civilians killed and 4 injured. Drone attacks targeting police stations and checkpoints increased, even in densely populated urban areas. Almost half of the day's attacks were on targets in the Tehran province. The Basij Command Headquarters and the IRGC garrison in Saadat Abad were hit, as were the Panahi Sports Complex, the Shahid Kazemi Sports Complex and two wrestling halls. Historic buildings in the Saadabad complex were damaged. The Shahid Ebadat garrison in Marivan and a missile site in Khomeini Shahr were also targeted. A strike hit the area of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, causing no damage. Several senior Iranian officials, including Ali Larijani, Basij chief Gholamreza Soleimani, and 300 Basij officials were killed by overnight Israeli airstrikes. Iran has not used its many anti-ship missiles, and USA used GBU-72 "bunker bombs" to target sites believed to contain such missiles.

target_text: Tehran
description: Esmaeil Khatib, Iran's Minister of Intelligence, was assassinated during overnight Israeli strikes in Tehran. Multiple explosions were reported in the early morning hours. Targets included naval zones and major port facilities, including areas around Shahid Bahonar and Shahid Rajaee ports, as well as near an oil terminal in Bandar Abbas. A strong explosion shook Ahvaz in the Zeytoun Karmandi area. An early-morning blast was reported in Songhor, a town close to the Iraqi border, while another explosion was heard near Kazerun in southern Iran. West of Tehran, multiple blasts occurred in Malard over a short time span. In Fardis, US-Israeli strikes targeted a helicopter manufacturing facility in the Naz industrial district. Fath Army Base in Karaj was hit by strikes, as were the town's welfare offices, rehabilitation center for persons with disabilities, and residential areas. Police stations in Qom and Bandar Lengeh were also hit.

Iran said that it targeted Shimon Peres Negev Nuclear Research Center in response to the strikes on Natanz and Bushehr. A strike in Arad wounded 116 people, seven seriously.

description: Iran fired ballistic missiles at the US-UK Diego Garcia military base in the British Indian Ocean Territory (BIOT) — the most distant target Iran has struck since the war began. The missiles did not hit the base per initial reports. Diego Garcia hosts a major US Air Force and Navy base (Camp Justice/Naval Support Facility Diego Garcia) and is a key staging ground for US operations in the region. The attack marks Iran extending its strike envelope into the Indian Ocean (~4,400 km from Iran). Sources: Times of Israel Day 22 liveblog (March 21, 2026) — featured headline; Wikipedia 2026 Iran war infobox (March 21, 2026) — location now includes "Indian Ocean."

target_text: Dimona Nuclear Research Center Area — Iranian Ballistic Missile Strike (Day 22)
description: Iran explicitly targeted the Dimona Negev Nuclear Research Center in southern Israel on Day 22 (March 21, 2026 — Eid al-Fitr), stating it was a direct response to the earlier US-Israeli strike on the Natanz enrichment facility. A 12-year-old boy was seriously wounded and approximately 34 others were injured (Euronews reports up to 40) when the missile struck without the boy reaching a bomb shelter. A mother confirmed to Kan public broadcaster that her son had not reached the shelter in time. Six additional injured were treated in surgical emergency room. Israel Health Ministry reported 150 people taken to hospitals in the past 24 hours due to conflict injuries. Iran declared it was targeting Dimona specifically as a "response" to the Natanz strike. The IDF had stated it was not responsible for striking Natanz (attributing that to the US). The Dimona Negev Nuclear Research Center is Israel's primary nuclear research facility and is widely believed to be the center of Israel's nuclear weapons program. This marks the first confirmed Iranian missile strike explicitly targeting Dimona during the 2026 conflict.

target_text: East Jerusalem (Al-Aqsa area) — Iranian Missile Debris Causes Extensive Damage (Day 22)
description: Missile fragments from an Iranian ballistic missile fell in occupied East Jerusalem on Day 22 (March 21, 2026 — Eid al-Fitr / Nowruz), landing just 350 metres (380 yards) from the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound and causing extensive damage to the religiously significant site. The missile was part of Iran's 9-salvo barrage at Israel on Day 22. No immediate casualty figures reported at the site. The Al-Aqsa Mosque compound is the third holiest site in Islam. The damage on Eid al-Fitr provoked immediate international outrage. Sources: Al Jazeera Day 22 explainer (March 21, 2026); Al Jazeera liveblog Day 22 (March 21, 2026).

target_text: IRGC Ammunition Depot — Dezful, Khuzestan Province (Day 22)
description: Combined US-Israeli forces struck an IRGC ammunition depot in Dezful, Khuzestan Province (southwestern Iran) on Day 22 (March 21, 2026). Confirmed by OSINT account on X, cited in ISW/CTP Evening Special Report. Dezful is a key military city in southwestern Iran — home to the IRGC Khatam al-Anbiya Air Defense Base and significant IRGC ground force infrastructure. Khuzestan Province has been a target of sustained strikes throughout the conflict due to its concentration of IRGC military-industrial sites.

target_text: Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility — US-Israeli Strike (Day 22)
description: The US and Israel launched an attack on the Natanz uranium-enrichment facility on the morning of Day 22 (March 21, 2026 — Eid al-Fitr). Iran's Tasnim news agency confirmed the strike and stated no radioactive leaks occurred and residents near the site are not at risk. Natanz was one of the main targets in the June 2025 war and has been struck multiple times during the current conflict. This is the third confirmed major strike on the Natanz facility.

target_text: Rishon Lezion, Central Israel — Iranian Cluster Bomb Ballistic Missile (Day 22)
description: Iran fired a ballistic missile carrying a cluster bomb warhead at central Israel (Rishon Lezion) on Day 22 (March 21, 2026). Israeli rescue forces reported damage at three impact sites from the bomblets, including an empty daycare. No injuries were reported. Education Minister Yoav Kisch ordered the cancellation of Passover holiday camps across 18 municipalities in central Israel following the strike.
